name: Design, Innovation, and Prototyping
description: Design: Introduction design process as a strategic problem-solving process, Drivers of innovation, Industrial design; Methods of data collection, Synthesizing, analyzing, and representing the data; Making a good brief; Craftsmanship/ techno-aesthetic detailing; Human factors, Applied ergonomics; Nature inspired design, Responsible design, Nuances of design; Innovation: Theory of inventive problem solving: Double diamond process, lateral thinking; Creativity models and tools used to push the boundaries: mind maps/ fishbone diagrams, brainstorming techniques, double diamond process; Prototyping: Freehand sketch, Overview of 3D solid modeling (CAD), 3D scanning using laser scanners; 3D Printing: Fused deposition modeling, stereolithography; Familiarization with machining processes: Centering, drilling, and milling using tabletop reconfigurable CNC machines; Casting, molding, and vacuum forming; Wood crafting: wood turning and joint making, laser cutting; Electronics prototyping: PCB milling and soldering; Familiarization with joining processes: adhesives, riveting, and fasteners; Familiarization with finishing processes: abrasive grinding, sand blasting, painting; Power tools in fabrication.

name: Principles and Applications of Electrical Engineering
description: Introduction:

name: The World of Engineering
description: Introduction to Engineering: The past: Case studies of engineering marvels; The present: State-of-the-art practices in modern engineering; The future: Identifying and conceptualizing the problem; Model and prototypes: Scale aspects; Design standards; Frugal innovation and logical thinking: Overlaps and tradeoffs; Engineering by Design (Active Mode): C2C: Concept to Commissioning: Ideation and thinking; Engineering project proposal; Budgeting and procurement; From ideas to feasibility (Active Mode): Introduction to simulation engines and programming tools; Feasibility studies; Quantifying milestones; From feasibility to scaled models (Active Mode): Translating ideas and simulations to scaled models; Quantifying performances; Pitching (Active Mode): An elevator pitch to sell/attract investors.

name: Materials for the Future
description: History of materials: Briefly show how the use of materials has evolved from stone age to present Types of materials: the major properties of metals, ceramics, polymers, and composites will be described with brief explanation regarding origin of these properties Choice of Materials: properties of materials which make them suitable for particular applications like- Photoelectric materials Advanced Manufacturing, Tribology High Temperature Materials Materials for energy generation and storage Materials for carbon neutral future Smart materials – Self-cleaning, self-healing Nature-inspired materials and biomaterials Materials for automobile, aviation, defense, etc. Computational materials and AI/ML in Materials

name: Principles of Artificial Intelligence
description: Introduction: Historical Perspectives, Computational Complexity and Computational Infeasibility, Problem Solving, Sorting and Searching, Games and Adversarial Search; Stochastic Systems and Decision Making: Uncertainty in AI, Bayesian Networks, Learning & Inference, Decision Theory, Markov Decision Processes, Decision Trees, Knowledge Graphs, Expert Systems, Precision and Recall, Models of Learning: Supervised and Unsupervised learning (including, dimensionality reduction and clustering), Reinforcement learning, Neural Networks, Introduction to Deep Learning. AI Applications: GenAI, Computer Vision, Natural Language Processing, Speech, Robotics. Labs: Understand asymptotic complexity by examples, differentiating between sublinear, poly, exp, etc.; Implement search and adversarial search, eg. a full game tree; Implement decision trees, random forests, clustering; Intro to Pytorch, llama, and scikit-learn; Make a SLM; Object Detection and Classification (YOLO etc.).

name: Introduction to Design and Innovation
description: Understanding of the design process that underlies any design undertaking; Concept of ideal design, conceptualizing product, visualization, preliminary estimates, material selection, engineering economics, ergonomics and aesthetics; Other aspects of design such as risk and liability, ethics, and impact on society and the environment.

name: Introduction to Materials
description: Introduction to materials and their classification, Atomic bonding and Crystal Structures, Imperfections and strengthening mechanisms, Diffusion – steady and nonsteady state, Corrosion and degradation of materials, Properties and applications: Mechanical, Thermal, Electrical, Magnetic, Electronic, Biological, Chemical. These topics will be interspersed with several case studies and in-class experiments/demonstrations.

name: Digital Systems
description: Review of Combinational and Sequential logic: Logic minimization, multi-level circuits, decoders, encoders, multiplexers, demultiplexers; Combinational logic: Shannon’s decomposition theorem, Comparator design, Multiplier design, Timing in combinational circuits, Static and Dynamic hazards digital circuits; Sequential logic: Timing in flip-flops and registers, metastability; Finite state machine: Design and analysis of synchronous Mealy and Moore FSMs, Asynchronous FSMs; Basics of memory organization; Introduction to Verilog and VHDL, Basic constructs in Verilog, Implementing combinational circuits and FSMs using Verilog; Case study: A simple computer design.

name: Digital Systems
description: Review of combinational and sequential logic: Logic minimization, multi-level circuits, decoders, encoders, multiplexers, demultiplexers; Circuit implementation on Programmable array logic (PAL) and on field programmable gate array (FPGA), MOS implementation of circuits; Shannon’s decomposition theorem, datapath circuits including comparator design, multiplier design, etc., static and dynamic hazards digital circuits; Sequential logic: Timing in flip-flops and registers, metastability; Finite state machine: Design and analysis of synchronous Mealy and Moore FSMs; Basics of memory organization; Basics of computer organization through a simple processor design including instruction set architecture, mnemonics, assembly programming concepts, basics of processor organization, Introduction to microcontrollers; Introduction to Verilog and VHDL, various HDL programming constructs, FPGA programming.

name: Thermodynamics
description: Introduction and basic concepts Thermodynamics & energy; applications of thermodynamics; importance of dimensions and units; systems and control volumes; properties of a system; state and equilibrium; processes and cycles; temperature and the zeroth of thermodynamics; generic problem-solving technique in thermodynamics Energy, Energy transfer and general energy analysis Forms of energy; energy transfer due to heat, work and matter; mechanical forms of work; the first law of thermodynamics; energy conversion efficiencies; energy and the environment Properties of pure substances Phases of a pure substance; phase-change processes; property diagrams and tables; link between classical and statistical properties; equations of state Energy analysis of closed systems Moving boundary work; energy balance for closed systems; specific heats; internal energy, enthalpy and specific heats for ideal gases Mass and energy analysis of control volumes Conservation of mass; flow work and the energy of a flowing fluid; energy analysis of flow systems; steady flow systems; unsteady flow processes The second law of thermodynamics Thermal energy reservoirs; heat engines, refrigerators and heat pumps; reversible and irreversible processes; internally reversible processes; Carnot cycle and principles; Carnot heat engine and refrigerator Entropy Clausius inequality; increase of entropy principle; entropy change of pure substances; property diagrams; isentropic processes; reversible steady-flow work; isentropic efficiency for steady flow devices; entropy balance Introduction to thermodynamic cycles Introduction to Rankine, Diesel and Brayton cycles for power production, mobility and aviation; introduction to vapor compression refrigeration cycle Exergy Work potential of energy; reversible work and irreversibility; second-law efficiency; exergy change of a system; exergy balance for closed systems and control volumes Thermodynamics of radiation Planck’s law for black bodies; Wien’s displacement law; absorption and emission of radiation; black bodies; Stefan-Boltzmann law Fundamentals of photovoltaic energy conversion Properties of sunlight; solar cell operation

name: Fluid Mechanics
description: Introduction to fluids, Continuum approximation, Knudsen number, properties of fluids, Fluid statics, Description of flows, Deformation of fluid elements, vorticity and irrotational flows, Reynolds’ transport theorem, Conservation of mass, Linear Momentum balance, Navier-Stokes’(NS) equation, Bernoulli equation and applications including flow measurement, Similitude and modeling, Non-dimensionalisation of NS equations, Importance of Reynolds number (Re), High Re flow: Prandtl’s approximation, basic inviscid flow, elementary plane flows and their superposition, Magnus effect, Boundary layers- elementary results for flat plates, Boundary Layer Solutions, Notion of Flow Separation. Momentum integral equation. Displacement and Momentum Thickness. Laminar and Turbulent Boundary Layers. Power Laws. Skin friction coefficient and drag estimation. Skin friction lines on surfaces. Flow through packed beds and fluidized beds; Transportation and metering of fluids, pump types, pump curves, blowers and compressors; Mixing and Agitation, power consumption, impeller types and flow patterns, mixing times.

name: Discrete Mathematics
description: Set, relations, equivalence relations, functions, countable and uncountable sets, Cantor’s diagonalization; Logic and Proofs, propositional logic, predicates and quantifiers, rules of inferences, proof techniques; Mathematical induction; Counting, permutations, combinations; Basic Discrete Probability; Elementary number theory, GCD, Euclid’s algorithm, Finite fields of prime order and application to hashing; Graph Theory, basic definitions, connectivity, tree, planarity, graph coloring; Pigeonhole principle, Ramsey theory; Inclusion-Exclusion; Basic group theory, Lagrange’s theorem, Euler’s theorem, application to RSA; Polynomial rings, finite field constructions and secret sharing.

name: Computer Organization and Architecture
description: Introduction to Computer Organization; Introduction to Instruction Set Architecture (ISA); RISC vs. CISC; Performance Metrics; Instruction Representation in Computers; Addressing Modes; Computer Arithmetic; Introduction to Assembly Programming; Processor Architecture; Pipelining Basics, In-Order and Out-of-Order pipelines; Data and Control Hazards; Exception Handling; Cache Basics; Cache Hierarchies; Cache Coherence; Virtual Memory; Memory Hierarchy; Multiprocessor Systems; SMT (Simultaneous Multi-Threading); SIMD/MIMD (Single/Multiple Instruction Multiple Data), GPUs; Synchronization and Consistency; Storage and I/O; Disks and Flash Memory; Performance Benchmarking; Warehouse Scale Computing.

name: Signals, Systems and Networks
description: Motivation and Basic Preliminaries of Signals and Systems; classification of signals in continuous and discrete cases, Basic signals — unit impulse, unit ramp, exponential (real and complex) functions, significance of basic signals; basic operations on signals; classifications of systems — concepts of linearity, causality, stability, time-invariance, convolution of CT and DT signals and systems; differential and difference equations, Fourier-domain analysis of CT and DT for periodic and aperiodic signals and systems — CTFS, CTFT, DTFS and DTFT and interrelations amongst them; Laplace transform; Introduction to Sampling and Z-transform; Elements of graph theory - incidence matrix, loop matrix, cut-set matrix, circuit analysis, twoport networks.

name: Mechanics of Solids
description: Free body diagram, Modeling of supports, Conditions for Equilibrium, Friction Force- deformation relationship and geometric compatibility (for small deformations) with illustrations through simple problems on axially loaded members and thin walled pressure vessels, Axial force, shear force, bending moment, and twisting moment diagrams of slender members, Concept of stress and strain at a point, Transformation of stresses and strain at a point, Principal stresses and strains, Mohr’s circle (only for plane stress and strain case), Displacement field, Strain Rosette, Modeling of problem as a plane stress or plane strain problem, Discussion of experimental results on 1-D material behavior. Concepts of elasticity, plasticity, strain-hardening, failure (fracture/yielding), idealization of 1-D stress-strain curve, Concepts of isotropy, orthotropy, anisotropy, Generalized Hooke’s law, (without and with thermal strains), Notions of elasticity, Torsion of circular shafts and thin-walled tubes, Bending of beams with symmetric cross-section (normal and shear stresses),Combined stresses, Yield criteria, Deflection due to bending, Integration of the moment-curvature relationship for simple boundary conditions, Super position principle, Concepts of strain energy and complementary strain energy for simple structural elements (those under axial load, shear force, bending moment, and torsion), Castigliano’s theorems for deflection analysis and indeterminate problems, Concept of elastic instability and a brief introduction to column buckling and Euler’s formula.

name: Data Structures and Algorithms I
description: REVIEW OF C PROGRAMMING, THE C ABSTRACT MACHINE, SPECIFICATION OF COMPUTATIONAL PROBLEMS AND ALGORITHMS, UNIT-COST RANDOM ACCESS MEMORY (RAM) MODEL, TIME AND SPACE COMPLEXITY, ASYMPTOTIC NOTATION, RECURRENCE ANALYSIS, PROOF OF CORRECTNESS AND PROOF OF EFFICIENCY, REVIEW OF SEARCHING, SORTING, AND SELECTION; ABSTRACT DATA TYPES: STACK AND QUEUES, IMPLEMENTATION OF STACK AND QUEUES USING ARRAYS, LINKED LISTS, RING BUFFERS; ABSTRACT DATA TYPE: PRIORITY QUEUE, IMPLEMENTATION OF PRIORITY QUEUES USING HEAPS, HEAPSORT; ABSTRACT DATA TYPE: SETS, DYNAMIC ARRAYS, AMORTIZED ANALYSIS, IMPLEMENTING SETS USING HASH TABLES, OPEN AND CLOSED HASHING: CHAINING, IMPLEMENTING SETS USING BINARY SEARCH TREES (BST), BST TRAVERSAL: PREORDER, INORDER, POSTORDER; ABSTRACT DATA TYPES: TREES AND GRAPHS, REPRESENTING GRAPHS USING ADJACENCY MATRICES AND ADJACENCY LISTS, DEPTH-FIRST SEARCH (DFS), BREADTH-FIRST SEARCH (BFS), AND ITERATIVE DEEPENING; GRAPH ALGORITHMS: SHORTEST PATHS, MINIMUM SPANNING TREES, UNION-FIND DATA STRUCTURE, STRONGLY CONNECTED COMPONENTS, TOPOLOGICAL SORTING; COMBINATORIAL ALGORITHMS: GENERATING ALL {PERMUTATIONS, SUBSETS, K-TUPLES, K-SUBSETS, PARTITIONS, K-PARTITIONS}; REDUCTIONS: LP, SAT, AND ILP SOLVERS.

name: Biology for Engineers
description: Introduction to living world – Molecules of life; information transfer in living systems; biological machines; cellular organization; molecular level data Bioinformatics and Computational Data analysis of biological systems – Data in Biology: Source, Scale, Storage and Services; Biological Sequence Analysis: Algorithms and concepts; Structure and folding of biomolecules; Visualization and modeling molecular interactions; Big data in molecular biology: Algorithms and concepts Emerging trends in Bio-engineering - 3D bioprinting including tissue engineering, biochips and biosensors; Vaccines and therapeutics

name: Signals, Systems, and Random Processes
description: Basics of Signals and Systems - Classification and Properties in Time Domain; Time Domain Analysis - Continuous and Discrete Time Linear Time Invariant (LTI) Systems, Linear Differential and Difference Equations, Characteristic Modes, Natural and Forced Responses, Zero Input and Zero State Responses, Convolution, Stability and Causality, Applications to Circuit Analysis; Transform Domain Analysis - Transforms and Properties - Continuous Time Fourier Series (CTFS), (CTFT), Laplace Transform, DiscreteTime Fourier Series (DTFS), Discrete Time Fourier Series (DTFT), Z Transform, Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT), Frequency Response, Gibbs Phenomenon, Parseval’s Theorem, Dirichlet Conditions, Stability, Sampling, Applications to Analog Communication Systems; Random Signal Analysis - Random Vectors, Central Limit Theorem, Law of Large Numbers, Random Processes, Classification, Examples, Wide Sense Stationarity (WSS) and Strict Sense Stationarity (SSS), Independent Identically Distributed (IID) Processes, Ergodicity, Autocorrelation and Power Spectral Density, Applications; WSS Random Signals through LTI Systems - Autocorrelation and Power Spectral Density, Gaussian Processes, White Gaussian Noise, Brownian Motion, Auto Regressive, Moving Average, Poisson Processes, Applications to Networks, Detection Theory, and Communication Channels.

name: Control Systems
description: Review of basic concepts: Notion of feedback; open- and closed-loop systems; Review of Laplace transforms, transfer functions, conversion of models between time domain and frequency domain; Modelling and representations of control systems: Ordinary differential equations, state-space representations, transfer function and block diagrams; Linearity, time-invariance versus time-variance; Linearization; Canonical forms; Dynamic response and stability of systems; Time-domain analysis, first order systems, second-order systems, standard responses – Impulse, step, ramp; Poles and zeros and their effects on responses; Dynamic response from the state equations, definition of stability, characteristic-equation, and roots; BIBO Stability, Routh-Hurwitz criteria; Design of controllers for SISO systems: Feedback control systems: Basic idea of feedback control systems, stabilizing control, regulation and tracking; Steady state error analysis; P, PI, PD, PID controllers, Ziegler–Nichols Tuning of the PID Controller; the root-locus technique; Design of controllers using root-locus; Feedforward control by plant model Inversion; Design of controllers for MIMO systems: Control-Law design for full-state feedback, pole placement with state feedback, controllability; Pole placement with output feedback, observability, Luenberger observer; LQR control; Frequency domain analysis and design: Frequency responses; Bode-plots; Gain-margin and phase-margin; Nyquist plots; Stability analysis in frequency domain, design via frequency response: Lead-lag compensators.

name: Vacuum Technology Laboratory
description: Introduction to vacuum systems and accessories (vacuum chambers, view ports, gaskets; Corrugated hose, KF clamps and accessories; valves, feedthroughs; pressure gauges; pumping system and accessories; Leak and outgassing measurement systems; Pumping speed measurement using roughing and the turbo molecular pumps; Estimating material permeability of various materials (SS 316, aluminum, glass, Kapton, copper, brass, etc.); Outgassing rate measurement for various materials/components (plastic foil, organic sample, polished and rough inorganic samples, porous materials, etc.); Designing and assembling vacuum systems (low vacuum, high vacuum, ultra-high vacuum systems); vacuum-based deposition/growth experiments and demonstrations

name: Integrated Circuit Fabrication Processes
description: Introduction to CMOS technology and CMOS process flow; Semiconductor materials, Silicon crystal growth and Wafer preparation; Introduction to cleanroom and utilities needed for semiconductor manufacturing; Front end of line (FEOL) process steps: Photolithography; Thermal oxidation (Dry Oxidation, Wet Oxidation); Thin film deposition (Chemical Vapor Deposition (CVD), Physical Vapor Deposition (PVD), Plasma Assisted Deposition, Atomic Layer Deposition (ALD) and Epitaxy); Etch (Wet Etch, Dry Etch); Chemical-Mechanical polishing (CMP); Ion Implantation; Back end of line (BEOL) process steps: Interconnects; Damascene Process; Electroplating; Introduction to Packaging; Process Integration: High-K/Metal-Gate Process; Process Induced Strain; Laser/Flash Annealing; FinFET process flow; Nanosheet FET Process Flow

name: Introduction to Data Structures and Algorithms
description: Basics of C and Data Structures: Data types: primitive and user-defined data types in C/C++, including those portable types used in embedded systems; Arrays, Pointers and Strings, Functions; Stacks, Queues, Linked List; Memory Management: Allocation, De-allocation, Memory Layout; Time and Space Complexity; Data Processing: Sorting and Searching; Data Handling and Processing: File Handling and Input/Output Handling, Files vs memory: buffering, streams, Standard Input/Output in C (fopen,fread, fwrite, fprintf, scanf pitfalls), Hands-on: implement reading/writing large files efficiently. Working with Structured and Unstructured Data: Handling Comma Separated Values/Tab Separated Values and binary tabular data in C, String parsing for unstructured text., Introduction to record-oriented storage (fixed vs variable length).

name: Data Structures and Algorithms II
description: Asymptotic notation (review); Recurrences; Divide and conquer : Quick sort, Merge sort, Linear-time Median finding, Strassen's matrix multiplication, Closest Pair; Greedy and Dynamic Programming, Graph Algorithms : Network flows and applications; Introduction to complexity classes; NP-completeness reductions; Number-theoretic Algorithm; Intermediate data structures: Height balanced trees, Amortized Analysis. Optional specialized topics: String algorithms, randomized algorithms (like min-cut) other models of analysis such as streaming/online/dynamic. Linear Programming. Dealing with intractability: Approximation Algorithms, Exact Algorithms etc.

name: Heat and Mass Transfer
description: Basic Concepts of Heat Transfer: Modes and laws of heat transfer, Conduction, heat transfer through extended surfaces, concept of resistance, Convection, boundary layer, heat transfer coefficient, overall heat transfer coefficient, LMTD; forced convection; natural convection; boiling and condensation; radiation; Heat Exchangers: Classifications and applications of heat exchangers, fouling factor, basic concepts of heat exchanger design, Kern method, NTU methods, design considerations for heat exchangers; Diffusion; Interphase mass transfer: theories of interphase mass transfer, local and overall mass transfer coefficients, correlations; analogy between momentum, heat and mass transfer;

name: Dynamics And Vibrations
description: Newtonian dynamics of a particle, systems of particles and of a rigid body; Force, torque, impulse, momentum, angular momentum, energy, and vibrations; Two-dimensional rigid-body kinematics including motion relative to a moving frame; Brief introduction to three dimensional rigid-body dynamics; Single degree of freedom system; Free and forced vibrations (harmonic and general), types of damping; Duhamel’s integration; Two degree of freedom system; Modal analysis, diagonalisation, eigensystem, response calculations for general excitation, proportional damping; Principle of virtual work, Lagrange’s equations.

name: Probability and Random Processes
description: Review of sets, fields and events, axioms of probability, probability space, conditional probability, independence, Bayes’ theorem and applications; repeated trials, Bernoulli trials; discrete, continuous and mixed random variables, probability mass function, probability distribution and density functions; examples of common random variables and density functions; conditional distributions and densities; functions of one and two random variables; moments and characteristic functions of random variables, mean, variance, correlation; Markov, Chebychev and Chernoff bounds; sequences of random variables, strong and weak law of large numbers, central limit theorem; linear mean square estimation and orthogonality principle; maximum likelihood and parameter estimation. Random processes, strict and wide sense stationary processes; ergodic processes; bandlimited and periodic processes; random processes and linear systems; power spectral density; noise processes; Wiener filtering; Kalman filtering; examples of random processes, Poisson process, Markov process.

name: Control Theory
description: Basic concepts: Notion of feedback; open- and closed-loop systems. Modeling and representations of control systems: Ordinary differential equations; Transfer functions; Block diagrams; Signal flow graphs; State-space representations, Performance and stability: Time- domain analysis; Second-order systems; Characteristic-equation and roots; Routh-Hurwitz criteria, Frequency domain techniques: Root-locus methods; Frequency responses; Bode-plots; Gain-margin and phase-margin; Nyquist plots; Compensator design: Proportional, PI and PID controllers; Lead-lag compensators. State-space concepts: Controllability; Observability; pole placement result; Minimal representations.

name: Microprocessors and Embedded Systems
description: Review of simple computer design; Brief comparison of RISC and CISC architectures, concepts of pipelining and hazards, memory; ARM Processor overview, architecture, instruction set, assembly/C language programming Memory/Peripheral bus, Interrupts, Direct Memory access (DMA), Digital I/Os; Peripheral interfacing – Timers, Universal asynchronous-synchronous transfer (UART), Serial peripheral interface (SPI), Inter-integrated circuit (I2C), Interfacing ADC/DAC (analog to digital converter and digital to analog converter)

name: Interface Design
description: · Introduction: Evolution of interfaces, Nuances of interfaces, Man machine interaction diagram · Effective Communication: Intent of communication, Modes of presentation(language.gesture.signs), Mental processes, Communication gaps, Emotional and Cultural aspects of communication · Elements of interfaces: Types of controls, Types of displays, Gesture control, Role of sensors, Light and sound, emergency and warning signs · Applied ergonomics: Physical (hand reach, RSIs), Cognitive aspects, Organizational · Principles of visual ergonomics: Visual hierarchy, Grouping, Color coding, Placement and orientation · Methodology: User persona, Scenario building, Opportunity mapping, Information management, prototyping and testing of interfaces

name: Machine Learning
description: Learning Foundations: Introduction and applications, learning paradigms, bias-variance tradeoff, empirical risk minimization, generalization, overfitting; Supervised Learning: linear and logistic regression, SVMs, decision trees, ensemble methods; Optimization: gradient descent, stochastic gradient descent, convexity, constrained optimization; Unsupervised Learning: PCA, k- Means, NMF, hierarchical clustering; Deep Learning: MLPs, CNNs, RNNs, autoencoders, autograd; Reinforcement Learning: conceptual introduction, Q-learning, value/policy intuition.

name: Computer Organization & Architecture
description: Introduction to Computer Organization; Introduction to Instruction Set Architecture (ISA); RISC vs. CISC; Performance Metrics; Instruction Representation in Computers; Addressing Modes; Computer Arithmetic; Introduction to Assembly Programming; Processor Architecture; Pipelining Basics, Data and Control Hazards; Exception Handling; Cache Basics; Cache Hierarchies; Cache Coherence; Virtual Memory; Memory Hierarchy; Multiprocessor Systems; SMT (Simultaneous Multithreading); SIMD/MIMD (Single/Multiple Instruction Multiple Data), Storage and I/O; Disks and Flash Memory; Performance Benchmarking; Warehouse Scale Computing.

name: Energy Systems
description: System tools for energy systems; economic tools for energy systems; Conventional Power Generation Technology (Fundamentals of Energy Conversion; Multi-species mixtures, chemical potential, Gibbs potential, reacting flow thermodynamics, Fuel Combustion and Gasification; Steam Power Plant Technology; Gas Turbine Power Generation Technology; Gas Turbine-Based Combined-Cycle Power Plants; Nuclear Power Plants; Co- and tri- generation; environmental impact mitigation of combustion based power generation) Renewable and Emerging Clean Energy Systems; Solar Thermal Energy; Solar Photovoltaic; Hydro-Power, Wind, Geothermal and Biomass Energy Systems; Electrochemical, Thermal and Mechanical Energy storage; Oxyfuel Combustion, Carbon Capture and Storage, Cleaner Coal Technologies; Emerging Clean Energy Technologies) Additional contents for ES 632 (one additional lecture hour or project engagement on average) Detailed thermodynamic modeling and Thermal management of electrochemical energy systems; refrigeration technologies VARS and VCRS systems; liquefaction; storage and transport of gaseous energy carriers; additional project on energy systems design

name: Process Data Analytics, Optimization, and Control
description: 1. Basics of optimization: variables, constraints, objectives, linear, nonlinear, mixed-integer, convex, nonconvex, formulation and solution using computer solver 2. Basics of process control: proportional, proportional-integral, and proportional-integral-derivative controllers, dynamic & multi-variable process modeling using computer software, and closed-loop simulation under different controllers 3. Data analytics: outlier removal, dimensionality reduction, principle component analysis, feature selection, k-means clustering, hierarchical clustering, support vector machines, random forest classification 4. Data-driven modeling and optimization: design of experiments, sampling, response surface model, neural network model, validation, surrogate-based optimization 5. Model predictive control (MPC): introduction to MPC, data-driven state-space model development, MPC controller development using state-space model, closed loop simulation under MPC. The course will use datasets familiar to engineers, such as industrial process data, energy & environmental systems data, and semiconductor manufacturing data.

name: Integrated Circuit Fabrication Lab
description: Overview of cleanroom environment and classifications, Cleanroom utilities and infrastructure requirements, Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), Safety protocols and best practices, Gowning procedures, Laboratory documentation and in-lab record keeping; Microfabrication process experiments: Wafer preparation (cleaning steps and native oxide removal), Mask preparation using laser writer (Mask design), Photolithography (Spin coating, Pre-bake, UV exposure, Post-bake, Development), Physical vapor deposition (sputtering, evaporation) and process parameter optimization, Chemical vapor deposition and process parameter tuning, Wet etching (etch rate determination and testing etching profiles on various orientations of Silicon), determination of over etching, under etching and end point parameters, thermal oxidation (validation of Deal-Grove model and diffusion) and doping (pre-deposition and subsequent drive-in of impurities to form electrical junctions). Fabrication and testing of basic semiconductor devices: resistor, capacitor, diode and MOS capacitor (MOSCAP).

name: Introduction to Fire Engineering
description: Introduction – Different kinds of Fire and classifications; Fire Science – The Basic Principles; The Fundamental elements of Fire Protection Engineering; Building Materials and Design; Water Supply for Fire Protection; Fire Extinguishing Systems; Fire Alarm System; Special Occupancies and Hazards; Occupancies Hazard Classification System; Fire Flow Demand for Unsprinklered Facilities; Performance Based Fire Safety Design; Codes and Regulations

name: Networks and Complex Systems
description: Introduction to networks, empirical study of real world networks: Social networks, Technological Networks, Biological networks, Neural Networks, and Information networks; Basic concepts in graph theory, Network representation, Adjacency matrix and edge lists, weighted networks, directed networks, bipartite networks, planar networks, degree, paths and connectivity, graph Laplacian; Characterization of and measures on networks, degree centrality, degree distribution, Katz centrality, hubs and authorities, between-ness centrality, clustering coefficient, Modularity, homophily and assortative mixing. Analytical and computational tools in networks; Representation of network with gephi software package. Random networks, properties of random networks; Small world networks: Watts-Stogartz model, properties and real world examples; Scale free networks: Barabasi-Albert model, theoretical approaches, characteristics of power law behavior in scale free networks. Examples from citation network, cellular network, internet etc. Dynamical processes on networks, microscopic approaches to dynamical phenomena, master equation, mean field solutions; Disease spreading on networks, Basic compartmental models like: SIS, SIR and SEIR. Epidemics spreading on static and adaptive networks, with examples from SARS and H1N1; Sexual contact networks and HIV. Resilience and robustness of networks, Percolation phenomena and phase transitions, percolation on complex networks; damage and resilience in networks, coupled networks and targeted attacks, cascading failures in network.

name: Mechatronics
description: Elements of Mechatronics systems: overview of Instrumentation, Electromechanical, and Information Systems. Actuators: Mechanical, Electromechanical, Electrical, Fluid power and Thermal systems; Characteristics, study and analysis in time and frequency domains. Sensors and Transducers: characteristics, design and applications; Signal conditioning, A/D, D/A conversions. Miniaturization: Micromachining and fabrication processes, Introduction to MEMS and MOEMS, Technology and Applications. Industrial Data Networks: Inter-device communications and Data logging. Systems and Controllers: Process Controllers, Programmable Logic Controllers, DCS, SCADA, and CNC Systems; Introduction to Computer Integrated Manufacturing.

name: Introduction to Inverse Modelling in Physical Sciences
description: Review: Mathematical prerequisites; Nature of mathematical modeling in physical sciences; Classification of inverse problems: discrete (parameter estimation) and continuous inverse problems. Discretizing continuous inverse problems using basis functions. ; Nature of ill-posed problems. Examples of ill-posed problems in physical sciences ; Inverse solutions using least squares methods. Rank deficiency and ill-conditioning: Singular Value Decomposition and generalized inverse; covariance and resolution of the generalized inverse solution. Minimum norm inverse solutions. model resolution and information density matrices. ; Tikhonov regularization: resolution, bias and uncertainty in solution, discrepancy method. Higher order Tikhonov regularization-Other regularization techniques: Sparsity regularization, bounds constraints; Fourier techniques: regularization in Fourier space. ; Stochastic inverse theory, Bayesian inverse theory: multivariate normal case, Markov Chain Monte Carlo methods, Analyzing MCMC outputs. Using stochastic methods for identifying a host of acceptable models. ; Nonlinear inverse modeling: Occam's inversion.

name: Topics in Complexity Science
description: Introduction to complexity, information theory, complexity measures and scale. Discrete-time and continuous-time dynamical Systems, bifurcations and chaos. Agent based modeling and cellular automata. Network science - social network analysis: network Generation, networks and behavior. Fundamentals of game theory. Simulation and networks labs.

name: Optical Fiber Communication
description: Overview of optical communications; Planar optical wave guides, modes and the eigenvalue equation, single-mode and multimode fiber; Group velocity and material dispersion, inter-modal and intra-modal dispersion, attenuation, pulse dispersion, fiber bandwidth, dispersion management; wavelength division multiplexing (WDM) concepts, Signal degradation, attenuation, dispersion and its compensation; Optical sources, semiconductor lasers and light emitting diodes, structure, spectral and temporal properties, modulation schemes; Photo-detectors, structure, operation, quantum efficiency, responsivity, spectral and temporal response; Detection schemes, coherent and non-coherent detection, performance analysis; WDM components, splitters, isolators, circulators, fiber Bragg gratings (FBG), Fabry-Perot and thin-film filters; Optical amplifiers, erbium doped fiber amplifiers

name: Deep Learning
description: Fundamentals of Deep Learning: Mathematics for Deep Learning - Calculus, Matrices, and Probability. Classification and Regression Problems. Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP), Deep MLP and Backpropagation Algorithm. Implementation - Training, Validation, and Testing using Deep MLP. Optimization Techniques for Deep Networks - Gradient Descent, Stochastic Gradient Descent(SGD), Momentum, Adaptive Gradient (Adagrad), Adaptive Momentum (AdaM), Root Mean Square Propagation (RMSProp). Regularization Techniques for Deep Networks - L1/L2, Data Augmentation, Dropout, DropBlock, Batch Normalization. Deep Neural Networks: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Selected CNN Architectures and their Applications - LENet-5, VGG-16, AlexNet, GoogLENet, InceptionNet, ResNet. Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN), Selected RNN Architectures and their Applications - Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) Networks and its variants, PixelRNN. Generative Adversarial Networks (GAN), Selected GAN architectures and their Applications - Conditional GAN, CycleGAN, WassersteinGAN, StyleGAN, StackGAN, BigGAN. Advanced Deep Learning Tools and Techniques: Design and Implementation - 3D CNN, Transfer Learning, Domain Adaptation, Autoencoders, Attention Mechanism, and Transformers. Deep Learning on Graphs - Embeddings, Graph Neural Networks, and their Applications. Self Supervised Learning. Tips and Tricks to enhance the Performance of Deep Neural Network implementations. Recent Advances. Applications: Research domains - Sustainability, Computer Vision, Natural Language Processing, Web Mining, Signal Processing, Bioinformatics, Climate Modeling.

name: Introduction to Photonics
description: Basic properties of light and classical treatment of light-matter interaction; origin of refractive index, absorption, dispersion; basic optical phenomena - interference, diffraction, birefringence; Generation of light: basic operation of light-emitting diodes and lasers; Lab demonstrations of important temporal and spectral properties of these sources Controlling the properties of light to convey information; modulation of intensity, phase and polarization; electro-optic modulators, acousto-optic modulators, polarizers; splitters, combiners, multiplexers; Propagation of light through dielectric waveguide structures – conventional and unconventional optical fiber; rectangular waveguide structures; metal-dielectric waveguides; Detection of light: p-i-n photodiodes, avalanche photodiodes; quantum efficiency, responsivity, spectral and temporal response Applications in engineering and science such as optical fiber communication, optical sensing of physical quantities (displacement, speed, strain, vibration, temperature) and chemicals (high-resolution molecular spectroscopy to identify different gases), plasmonics, biophotonics, imaging, light detection and ranging (LIDAR) as used in distributed sensing and autonomous cars. Lab demonstrations of sensing displacement, vibration, temperature and gas leaks

name: Nature-inspired Materials Design
description: I. Preliminaries of biomimetic materials - Principles of biomimicry from the fields of biology, engineering and design - Processing and characterization of Biomimetic materials - Models and simulations of structure-property relationships in biomimetics

name: Space Science and Satellite Technology
description: Introduction to Astronomy and Space Physics – review of essentials of solar systems and its phenomena; Space weather- earth orbits and plasma environment, spacecraft charging and its mitigation techniques; Orbits and its dynamics- Keplerian orbits, Kepler’s laws, Orbital parameters, Useful orbits for satellite applications, Orbit Perturbations; Launch vehicles - Injection into orbit with a conventional launcher, Transfer phase, Positioning phase; Indian launch vehicles; Different segments of an artificial satellite- Space segments; power system, attitude and orbit control system, station keeping, thermal control, Telemetry, Tracking & Command (TT&C) subsystem, payloads, propulsion system; Earth segments; Reliability of satellite systems-The probability of survival or reliability, Failure probability or unreliability, mean time to failure; Mean satellite lifetime; Reliability during the wear-out period; Application of artificial satellites- Communication satellite; uplink, downlink and overall link performance; Intersatellite links, frequency allocations, different types of transponders; bent pipe, regenerative payloads, navigation satellite; Remote sensing; different types of remote sensing satellites, microwave payloads; optical payloads and interplanetary satellites.

name: 5G and Beyond: An Introduction
description: Overview of 5G: Evolution, standardization, application potential in IoT, AR/VR, Smart infra and Autonomous Vehicles. Core, RAN and air interface, protocol stack and layers; 5G physical layer, air interface and RAN: Millimeter wave propagation characteristics, Modulation and demodulation. multiplexing and multiple access. OFDM, OFDMA. Multiple antenna techniques, Air interface protocol stack, frequency and time domain structures, numerologies. radio interface and access protocol; 5G virtualization: Software defined networks, Architecture and Principles: Control and Data Planes, separation of control and data, SDN use cases. Network virtualization, NFV, service management and orchestration, Multi-access Edge computing in 5G and B5G networks: MEC Architecture, Mobility, service continuity, traffic routing; 5G Automation: Network Slicing, Requirements for slice automation and 5G packet core services. End-2-End network slicing and service Assurances. Cloud RAN Architecture, challenges and Non-public 5G networks.

name: Financial Modeling and Engineering
description: Financial markets and instruments, time value of money, forwards, futures, swaps, options and payoff structures, put–call parity, option strategies, binomial option pricing, Black–Scholes model, Greeks, volatility smile, stochastic volatility, jump models, fixed income securities, yield curve, duration, convexity, interest rate models including Vasicek, Hull–White, and CIR, credit risk, credit derivatives, portfolio theory, mean–variance optimization, Value at Risk (VaR), Conditional VaR, stress testing, Monte Carlo simulation, numerical methods, option pricing applications, real options, algorithmic trading, and FinTech applications

name: Numerical Methods for Engineers
description: Basic review of numerical methods and mathematical modeling in engineering through simple examples. Review of Taylor series and error estimates; Solutions of Linear and Nonlinear Systems: Review of LU decomposition, Singular value decomposition (SVD), Thomas algorithm, Review of Iterative methods, Successive Over-Relaxation (SOR); review of Newton-Raphson method and applications in engineering; Numerical differentiation: Review of curve fitting and regression, finite difference method - explicit and Implicit schemes, Crank-Nicolson and Alternating-direction implicit method; Numerical integration: Romberg integration, Newton-Cotes method and Gaussian quadrature; Numerical Solution of Ordinary Differential Equations (ODEs), Initial value problems: Runge-Kutta methods (RK2, RK4, embedded and adaptive), multi-step predictor-corrector methods: Adams-Bashforth, Adams-Moulton, Stability and convergence analysis, Boundary value problems: finite difference and collocation methods; Numerical Solution of Governing Equations in Engineering: Diffusion equation (parabolic), Laplace and Poisson equations (elliptic), advection-diffusion equations, Wave equation (hyperbolic), Eigen value problems, Stiff equations, Introduction to finite element and finite volume methods, applications in heat transfer, fluid mechanics, and structural analysis; Computational Methods in Engineering: Preconditioning and subspace methods, model order reduction techniques (multi-physics applications) – proper orthogonal decomposition, reduced basis method. Accelerated time integration methods, multiple time stepping and method of multiple scales

name: Industrial Control and Process Instrumentation
description: Principles of Industrial Automation: Building blocks of Instrument automation systems starting from Sensor/Transducer to Digital Interface, Data acquisition and control automation Introduction on Process Measurements: Level, Flow, Pressure and temperature Basic Principles, types , structures, equivalent circuits and characterization : Magnetic, Transformer, electrodynamic and Servo elements for measuring position velocity and acceleration; Mechanical, acoustical and Ultrasonic Elements: Dynamic characteristics of inertial mass elements, Vibrating beams and strings: Optical sensors and spectroscopy, General principles of chromatography: chromatographic behavior of solutes, quantitative determination, Gas analyzers and Pollution monitoring instruments: Signal Processing and Data Acquisition: Basics on Signal conditioning, filtering, amplification, Isolation, Linearization and calibration; conversion to 4-20mA standards; Spectral estimation, fault analysis, Functionalities of a Typical Data Acquisition (DAQ) module, I/O port configurations, Sampling and reconstruction, ADC and DAC operations of DAQ: Process Control Need for process control , Hierarchical decomposition of control functions, Continuous and batch processes , P&ID diagram , Self-regulation , Interacting and non-interacting systems - Mathematical model of Level, Flow and Thermal processes: Characteristic of ON-OFF, P, P+I, P+D and P+I+D control modes : Digital PID algorithm : Dynamic behavior of Feedback-Controlled Processes: Evolution of PLC: Architecture, Programming of PLC , Relay and Ladder logic , Functional blocks , Communication networks, smart sensors and Field bus. SCADA: Remote terminal units, Master station, Communication architectures and Open SCADA protocols. Laboratory: Experiments based on the above contents.

name: RFIC Design
description: Introduction to RF and Transmission Line Theory covering signal behavior, reflection coefficients, VSWR, and impedance matching using the Smith Chart for lumped and distributed elements; RF Network Representation focusing on S-parameters for circuit characterization and shifting of reference planes; Wireless Architectures and CMOS Integration exploring transceiver blocks, IC design constraints, and the transition from discrete components to Silicon microelectronics; High-Frequency Transistor Modeling and Low Noise Amplifiers (LNA) focusing on f, T, fmax, and parasitic effects to implement inductively degenerated CMOS topologies with optimized noise figures; Mixer Design and Frequency Translation encompassing active Gilbert Cells and passive CMOS switching mixers while addressing conversion gain and port isolation; Oscillators and Frequency Synthesis covering negative resistance theory, LC and Ring Oscillator topologies, phase noise, and the basics of Phase Locked Loops (PLL); Amplifier and CMOS Power Amplifier (PA) Design focusing on stability, Class A-C operations, and efficiency (PAE) for large-signal performance; System Integration addressing transceiver challenges such as IQ imbalance, DC offsets, and the role of integrated filters in signal chains.

name: Semiconductor Foundry Engineering
description: Business Imperatives: CMOS Technology motivation & impact, the foundry ecosystem, value chain, Node Power/Performance/Area requirements; customer engagement (timeline, Ground Rule Validation and Design Rule Validation, Process Design Kit, Intellectual Property, Electronic Design Automation); Product Types (SOI, embedded processors, mobile application processors, servers, r.f.), Design Technology Co-Optimization, Cost Model; Fab Imperatives: Scaling roadblocks/remedies, process defect detection/classification/genesis, Automatic Process Control, stress engineering for enhanced performance, in-line metrology; Scribe Line Monitors, Risk Management (Failure modes and effects analysis), physical failure and root-cause analysis, Pareto/Yield Analysis, Statistical Process Control, device performance and reliability concerns including (Gate Oxide Integrity, Channel Hot Carriers, Gate Induced Diode Leakage, Latch-up, Electromigration, Radiation Hardening), multi-product engineering for a wide customer base; case studies for several sub-topics; Overall Imperatives: Supply Chain Management (supplier qualification, second source development, proactive stock-level monitoring), Environmental Impact Mitigation (recycling, local sourcing, carbon footprint, compliance), Final Testing (functional, parametric), Product Engineering, Continuous Improvement Programs.

name: Engineering Optimization
description: Introduction to Optimization; Formulation of Various Process Optimization Problems and their Classification; Basic Concepts of Optimization-Convex and Concave Functions, Necessary and sufficient conditions for Stationary Points; Optimization of one-dimensional Functions; Unconstrained Multivariable Optimization- Direct Search Methods. Indirect First Order and Second Order Methods; Linear Programming and its Applications; Constrained Multivariable Optimization-Necessary and Sufficient Conditions for Constrained Optimum, Quadratic Programming, Sequential Quadratic Programming; Optimization of Staged and Discrete Processes, Dynamic Optimization, Integer and Mixed Integer Programming. Additional topics such as convexification, Global Optimization, and mixed integer programming will be discussed.

name: Computational Neuroscience
description: Levels of Analysis, Neurons, Electric potential, membrane potential, neural activation function, excitation, inhibition, winner take all, constraint satisfaction, Hebbian learning, principal component analysis, Infomax, MDL, error driven learning, delta rule, backpropagation, sequence and temporally delayed learning, reinforcement learning, large scale brain function, structural and dynamic principles, vision, object recognition, spatial attention models, Hippocampal long term memory models, language processing models, higher level cognition.

name: Foundations of Fluid Dynamics
description: vector calculus, Introduction to tensors. Concept of continuum, Knudsen’s number Kinematics of fluid motion: Eulerian and Langarangian viewpoint. Basic laws: Continuity and momentum equations, Conservation Laws for Transport Processes (Mass. Momentum and Energy), Analysis of Stress in Viscous Fluids and Strain for Fluid Deformation, Constitutive Relation:, Navier-Stokes equations. , Examples of incompressible flow patterns.Vorticity dynamics: vorticity diffusion, intensification by stretching, production of vorticity, Helmholts law, Kelvins theorem. Motion of Vortex Lines, Vortex Filaments and Biot- Savart Law. Potential flow: Streamlines and velocity potential. Boundary layers, concept of boundary layers, displacement and momentum thickness, von Karman momentum integral, approximate methods. Blasius and Falkner-Skan similarity solutions, flow separation, axisymmetric boundary layers, free shear layers, jets. Introduction to turbulent flows, types and characteristics of turbulent flows, energy cascade, Kolmogorov scale, Reynolds decomposition, RANS equation, closure problem.

name: Nanoscale device Engineering
description: Introduction to nanotechnology, the size of things, history of nanotechnology, fabrication methods - top-down and bottoms-up, emerging applications of nanotechnology; Physics at the nanoscale, review of electrodynamics, overview of quantum mechanics and statistical mechanics, electrons and photons, wave-particle duality, electron in potential wells, tunneling, scattering of electrons and photons; Semi-classical treatment of light-matter interactions, Electron transport at the nanoscale - Moore’s law and device size scaling, fundamental limits of CMOS technology, field effect transistors, conventional MOSFET, ballistic FETs, FinFETs, single electron transistors, quantum dots photonics at the nanoscale, diffraction limit of light, optoelectronic integration, photonic crystals, surface plasmons, metamaterials, nanoantenna and optical circuits, enhanced light-matter interaction with nanoantennae; applications in sensors, energy harvesting, information processing, quantum computing.

name: Artificial Intelligence
description: Task Environment and its properties; Artificial Agent; Uninformed Search Strategies; Different types of Local Searches; Game Playing Theory; Propositional Logic and Inference Rules; Planning; Regression Planning; Planning in Real World; Decision Theory under Uncertainty; Machine Learning Techniques.

name: Fundamentals of Artificial Intelligence
description: Artificial Agent; Uninformed and Heuristic Search Strategies; Different types of Local Searches; Game Playing Theory; Planning, Knowledge representation using Logic and Rules; Bayes’ Theorem and Bayesian Networks; Connectionist models; Perception and Action to build Artificial Agents.

name: Modern Control Theory
description: Introduction to linear algebra, (Matrices, differential equations and states), linear time invariant systems and its solutions, mulitivariate systems introduction, state space analysis of continuous systems, controllability, observability, stability with respect to state space analysis, Conversion between transfer function and state space models, Lyapnov theorem for stability, full state back control design, pole placement and control, overview of important concepts in stochastic data analysis, Kalman filters for state estimation, Discussion on Kalman filter as Best Linear Unbiased Estimate (BLUE), Introduction to Model predictive control (MPC) for linear systems with emphasis on linear quadratic control; Advanced topics based on Time criteria: Case studies with emphasis on use of Kalman filters with MPC for state estimation and control.

name: Nature Inspired Computing
description: Introduction to neural networks, perceptrons, adaptive learning principles, multi-layer perceptrons (MLP), back propagation algorithm, radial basis function (RBF) networks, K- means clustering, functional link artificial neural networks (FLANN), Legendre neural networks, Chebyshev neural networks, support vector machines, Hebbian and Hopfield learning; Fuzzy Computing, fuzzy sets, membership functions, fuzzy rules, adaptive neuro-fuzzy inference systems (ANFIS); Introduction to single and multi-objective evolutionary algorithms, genetic algorithm (GA), binary and real coded GA, differential evolution (DE), types of DE, ant colony optimization (ACO) algorithm, particle swarm optimization (PSO) algorithm, variants of PSO, multi-objective GAs, non-sorting genetic algorithm-II (NSGA-II); Engineering applications of nature inspired computing, evolutionary robotics – an overview.

name: Digital Control Systems
description: Signal processing in digital control — Sampling, time and frequency domain description, Reconstruction; Modelling by difference equations and pulse Transfer function; Time response analysis of discrete time systems; Stability of Discrete time systems; Jury's stability test; State space models; Controllability and Observability; Lyapunov method of stability analysis, Lyapunov functions; Review of compensator design, digital compensator design using frequency response plots; Digital PID controller; Finite settling time design; Dead beat systems and inter-sample behaviour; State space approach for digital controller design — pole placement, output and state feedback, observer-based design — full order and reduced order; Dead beat controllers; Mechanization of control algorithms — Real time implementation of digital controllers.

name: Design of Experiments
description: Fundamentals: Fundamentals of statistics, introduction to probability, hypothesis testing; Design and analysis with Factorial structures & complete randomization: randomization, one factor at a time testing, factorial experiments in completely randomized design, Analysis of completely randomized design, ANOVA, Fractional factorial experiments and analysis; Design and analysis with random effects & special designs: Controlling experimental variability, two level and three level factorial experiments, analysis of experiments with random levels; Data analysis, regression and model assessment: Least square estimation, statistical significance of least squares, regression with multiple variables and covariates.

name: Advanced Solid Mechanics
description: Stress Analysis: Theory of stress, equilibrium equations, principal stresses and stress invariants. Analysis of Deformation: Lagrangian and Eulerian description, finite strain, small deformation theory, principal strains and strain invariants, compatibility conditions. Constitutive Theory: Generalized Hook’s law, linear elasticity relations, constitutive relations for orthotropic and anisotropic materials yield criteria, plastic potential and flow-rule. Two-dimensional problems: Plane stress and plane strain, Airy stress function, pressure vessels. Torsion: Non-prismatic sections, thick and thin-wall cylindrical shafts, shear centre and shear flow. Unsymmetrical bending. Thin Plates: Kirchhoff plate theory, rectangular plates. Buckling: rectangular plates. Class Project: Experimental / Numerical as decided by the Instructor.

name: Finite Element Methods
description: Introduction; Basic Steps, Preliminaries and Definitions; One-dimensional Stress-Deformation: Axial column; Computer Implementation; 1-D code for 3 above; Two-dimensional Stress Deformation; Computer Implementation; 2-D code for 5 above (continuing); One-dimensional (Steady) and Transient Flow: Uncoupled Analysis, Heat Flow, and Consolidation; Computer Implementation; 1-D code for 7 above; Beam Bending and Beam-Column; Two-dimensional Field Problems: Heat Flow, Fluid Flow, Torsion; Multicomponent (e.g., structure and foundation) Systems (depending on time and interest).

name: Biomolecular Materials Science
description: Structures and functions of basic classes of biomolecular materials – Nucleic acids, Proteins, Lipids & sugars; Specific & non-specific interactions between biomolecular materials – Electrostatic, van der Waals, hydrophobic interactions; Case studies in biomolecular materials science,– Tissue engineering, drug delivery, role of self-assembly in diseases.

name: Non- Linear Elasticity
description: Deals with large deformation elasticity tuned towards modeling cardiac muscle mechanics. Tensors Algebra, Tensor Analysis, Kinematics of deformation, material and spatial coordinates, deformation gradient tensor, nonlinear and linear strain tensors, strain displacement relations; balance laws, Cauchy and Piola Kirchoff stresses, Cauchy equations of motion, balance of energy, stored energy; constitutive relations, elasticity, hyperelasticity, choice of hyperelastic models, solution of selected problems, Muscle modeling (Active Stress/Active Strain approach), Numerical implementation.

name: Optical Communications
description: Overview of optical communications; Planar optical wave guides, modes and the eigenvalue equation, single-mode and multimode fiber; Group velocity and material dispersion, inter-modal and intra-modal dispersion, attenuation, pulse dispersion, fiber bandwidth, dispersion management; wavelength division multiplexing (WDM) concepts, Signal degradation, attenuation, dispersion and its compensation; Optical sources, semiconductor lasers and light emitting diodes, structure, spectral and temporal properties, modulation schemes; Photo-detectors, structure, operation, quantum efficiency, responsivity, spectral and temporal response; Detection schemes, coherent and non-coherent detection, performance analysis; WDM components, splitters, isolators, circulators, fiber Bragg gratings (FBG), Fabry-Perot and thin-film filters; Optical amplifiers, erbium doped fiber amplifiers

name: Microfabrication and Semiconductor Processes
description: Introduction to CMOS technology, Overview of semiconductor materials and devices, Crystal growth and silicon wafers, Front end processes: Cleaning, Thermal oxidation, Ion implantation, Diffusion, Lithography, Thin film deposition and Etching, Back-end processes: Metallization and Planarization, Packaging, Process integration, Layout design rules, MEMS structures and fabrication techniques, Introduction to design for manufacturing (DFM).

name: Linear Algebra and Computation
description: Vector spaces, linear independence, basis, inner product spaces: Applications in Fourier analysis of Boolean functions; Strassen Algorithm, Gaussian elimination: Computational issues; Computing determinant: combinatorial approach; Solving Linear Diophantine equations; Expander graphs and Eigenvalue, Raleigh quotient, Cheeger’s inequality; Factoring: Shor’s algorithm;SVD and it applications in machine learning.

name: Energy Systems
description: System tools for energy systems; economic tools for energy systems; Conventional Power Generation Technology (Fundamentals of Energy Conversion, Heat Transfer, and Fluid Mechanics; Fuel Combustion and Gasification; Steam Power Plant Technology; Gas Turbine Power Generation Technology; Gas Turbine-Based Combined-Cycle Power Plants; Nuclear Power Plants; Cogeneration and Trigeneration; Power Plants’ Environment Impact Control) Renewable and Emerging Clean Energy Systems; Solar Thermal Energy Technology; Photovoltaic Technology; Hydro-Power, Wind, Geothermal, Marine, and Biomass Energy Systems; Advanced Energy Storage; Oxyfuel Combustion, Carbon Capture and Storage, Cleaner Coal Technologies; Emerging Clean Energy Technologies)

name: Applied Multivariate Data Analysis
description: Review of basics of linear algebra, random variables, probability density functions, correlation function; Process modeling linear regression, nonlinear regression, ordinary and total least squares, principal component analysis (PCA), functional PCA, non-negative matrix factorization, independent component analysis, kernel PCA; Applications, parameter estimation in linear and nonlinear processes, data reconciliation, controller performance monitoring, fault diagnosis, biomedical and speech signal processing

name: Water Quality Engineering
description: Fundamental theory and application of the physical and chemical processes in water and wastewater treatment: Introduction to water quality parameters, standards, Fundamentals, optimization and design of the following processes for water treatment: Coagulation, Flocculation: Destabilization mechanism, pC-pH (coagulation) diagram, Flocculation kinetics, Sedimentation: Design equations for settling basins, Water Conditioning, Softening: Chemical Reactions for softening, Softening process design, Disinfection: Breakpoint chlorination, CT concept and inactivation kinetics, Reactor design: CSTR, batch, plug flow reactor equations, tracer tests Ozone contactor design: Transport model for contactor design, Air stripping: Design and apply equation for air stripping process, Membrane Processes: Membrane materials, module types, High Pressure Membrane Process: Nanofiltration, Reverse Osmosis - models and their application, Low Pressure Membrane Process: Microfiltration, Ultrafiltration - models and their application, Activated Carbon Adsorption Process: Adsorption isotherms, required carbon dose, Ion exchange: Types of ion exchange resin, ion exchange design equation, Design Project: Design a water purification system for a sea-side town with the results of field tests of several water supplies in the town given.

name: Mathematical Foundations for Computer Vision & Graphics
description: Numerical algorithms: linear systems and decompositions – LU, QR, singular value decomposition (SVD), eigenvalue problems, variational problems, numerical solutions to ODEs and PDEs; Numerical Optimization: nonlinear systems, unconstrained optimization, constrained optimization, iterative linear solvers, sparsity; Geometry: projective geometry, transformations, polygons and polyhedra, curves and surfaces, manifolds; Graphs and Networks: graphs basics, graph cuts, belief propagation, Markov random fields, conditional random fields, convolutional neural networks.

name: Multimedia Security
description: Introduction to Information Hiding, Steganography, Watermarking and Digital Rights Management (DRM), Applications and Properties; Models of Watermarking; Information Theoretical Analysis of Digital Watermarking; Digital Watermarking Schemes such as Spread Spectrum Watermarking, Watermarking with Side-Information; Media Specific Digital Watermarking; Digital Watermarking Protocols such as Buyer-Seller Watermarking Protocol, Extensions of Watermarking Protocols; Attacks and Benchmarks of Digital Watermarking Systems; Steganography & Steganalysis; Digital Forensics: Processing pipeline and sensor characteristics of multimedia generation devices, identifying origin, processing history, manipulation, information leakage, or steganographic content in audio, image, or video; Introduction to Privacy-Preserving Signal Processing.

name: Electronic Instrumentation
description: Instrumentation Basics (Sensor, Transducer, Standard Instrument Signals – Pneumatic, Current Loop, Voltage Loop, Instrument Properties – Range and Span, Resolution, Accuracy and Precision, Instrumentation Dynamics – Gain, Time Constant, Dead time); Data Acquisition (DAQ) systems – sampling and digitization; Basic Principles of Industrial Automation; Measurement of Flow, Pressure, Temperature, Strain, Level, Force; Non-invasive measurement techniques (e.g., ultrasonic, optical, etc.); Industrial Process and Instrumentation (P&I) used for Control of process parameters (e.g., Level, Temperature, etc.); P&I related Industrial Safety Issues (NEMA considerations for Enclosures); Communication Bus (HART, Profibus, Fieldbus).

name: Control of Nonlinear Dynamical Systems
description: A review of vector spaces; phase portraits of second-order systems; ordinary differential equations (ODEs), the Lipschitz condition and existence and uniqueness of the solution; affine- in- thecontrol nonlinear systems; linearization and the Jacobian; flows, stability and invariant sets; Lyapunov theorem and proof; Preliminaries on a compact set and LaSalle's invariance theorem; non-autonomous systems, Lyapunov theorem extensions and class KL functions; operations on vector fields - the gradient, the Lie derivative and the Lie bracket; distributions, feedback linearization, zero dynamics and controller design examples.

name: Optimization Methods for Machine Learning
description: Motivation & Introduction; Structures of optimization problems arising during supervised and unsupervised learning such as SVM, Logistics Regression, Linear Regression, Kernel Methods, Sparse Modelling, Artificial Neural Networks; Smooth Convex Optimization: Convex Sets, Convex Functions, Gradient Descent Methods, Stochastic Gradient Descent Methods, Nesterov’s Method, Projected Gradient Methods; Non-smooth Convex Optimization: Subgradient and Proximal Methods, Shrinkage Methods; Duality Theory: Lagrangian Dual, Wolfe Dual, Fenchel Dual; Lagrangian Relaxation: Dual Decomposition, Augmented Lagrangian Methods; Large-Scale Learning: Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM), Parameter Mixing, Non-decomposable Loss Functions.

name: Elastodynamics and Vibrations
description: Vibration of discrete systems: Variational approach to derive the equations of motion (EOM), equilibrium points and linearization about an equilibrium point, forced response due to arbitrary excitations (Duhamel/ convolution integral), modal analysis and decoupling of EOMs. Vibration of continuous systems: Vibration of strings, bars, beams (Rayleigh beams, EulerBernoulli beams and Timoshenko beams) and membranes. Variational approach to derive the EOM, modal analysis, Sturm-Liouville boundary value problem, orthogonal functions and orthogonality conditions, expansion theorem, initial and boundary value problems, forced response of systems, time dependent boundary conditions, D'Alambert's wave solution, solution by Green's function approach and by integral transforms, approximate methods (Ritz method, Rayleigh-Ritz method, Rayleigh quotient, Galerkin's method).

name: Nonlinear Dynamics and Vibrations
description: Introduction to nonlinear systems with examples from mechanical, electrical, biological and chemical systems, theorems of existence and uniqueness of solutions for general class of nonlinear oscillators, dynamics in phase space, stability and classification of fixed points, general types of orbits; Basic asymptotic/perturbation methods for analyzing the free and forced responses of single- and multi-degree-of-freedom nonlinear oscillators; Forced (fundamental, sub-harmonic and super-harmonic) response, internal and combination resonances in nonlinear systems, and linearized stability analysis; Floquet theory for linear parametrically excited systems, and stability of periodic solutions; Introduction to discrete-time dynamical systems (nonlinear maps) and Poincare' maps; Theory of bifurcations in nonlinear systems.

name: Computational Inelasticity
description: One dimensional plasticity a_nd viscoplasticity; the elastoplastic problem; Multi-dimensional plasticity, thermodynamics and dissipation; Integration algorithms for plasticity and viscoplasticity; general return mappmg algorithms (operator splits, closest-point projection); consistent linearization; Nonlinear stability of integration algorithms for plasticity and viscoplasticity; Hypo-elastic plasticity models; objective integration algorithms; Multiplicative finite strain plasticity and Integration algorithms.

name: Introduction to Electrical Systems
description: Source and transport of pollutants, Mass balance and ecosystem dynamics, Balance laws and constitutive relationships, Source functions of pollutants Redox Processes: Redox Diagram and stability of water, Dissolved species and gases with special emphasis on arsenic, nitrate, iron and methane in groundwater Sorption of Trace Metals: Origin and occurrence of metal, Sorption isotherms and distribution coefficient, variable charge surface, surface complexation and kinetics Organic Contamination: Transport, sorption and transformation of organic chemicals, Monod biotransformation kinetics, Fate of hydrophobic chemicals Geogenic Contamination: Arsenic, Fluoride, Iron and Uranium pollution in the groundwater. Soil pollution, Fractionation, Bioavailability, Relative absorption factor (RAF), Biochemical exchange in atmospheric, aquatic, and terrestrial domains. Basics of Contaminant Transport in Natural System, Surface Flows, Unsaturated/Saturated Porous Media, Multiphase flow systems and in piped systems Contaminant Transport Modelling: River Water Quality Modelling, Lake Water Quality Modelling, Groundwater Quality Modelling , Pipe network modelling. Remediation technology, Reactive barriers, Biological technologies, Bioremediation, Remediation optimization, Emerging technologies, Remediation trend

name: Nonlinear Continuum Mechanics
description: Review of Vector and Tensor algebra, Kinematics: configurations, material and spatial derivatives, finite deformation theory, deforrnation gradients a_nd polar decomposition, Concept of stress: traction vectors and stress tensors, alternative stress tensors, Balance principles: Conservation of mass, Reynolds tra_nsport theorem, Momentum balance, continuum thermodynamics, entropy Inequality, Objectivity and objective rates, thermodynamics of materials: isothermal and isentropic elasticity tensors, Internal variable approach, kinds of materials and constitutive theories, introduction to variational pnnciples

name: Medical Imaging Systems
description: The goal of this course is to provide an overview of standard clinical imaging modalities from the signals and systems perspective. Ultrasound, X-ray, Computer Tomography (CT), and Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) will be covered. The course will address image acquisition and reconstruction for respective imaging modalities, choice of the appropriate modality for specific applications, modality- specific issues on image quality, clinical applications, and safety- related considerations for each approach. The students will be introduced to metrics for assessing imaging performance and diagnostic value. Examples from classic and current literature will be discussed. The following topics will be covered: 1. Review of linear systems, 2-dimensional Fourier Transform, electromagnetic spectrum, acoustic spectrum, attenuation, scattering 2. Ultrasound imaging: Acoustic propagation, reflection, diffraction, attenuation, Doppler effect, tissue properties, speckle, transducer probes, devices, image artifacts, safety 3. Conventional X-ray: X-ray sources, tissue properties, artifacts, noise/resolution 4. Computed tomography: Projection, central-slice theorem, image reconstruction, backprojection, filtering, convolution, noise, artifacts, safety 5. 5. Magnetic Resonance Imaging: Larmor frequency, tissue properties, rotating frame, gradients, excitation pulses, Fourier transform relations, devices, artifacts, and safety

name: Human-Robot Interaction
description: Introduction: Human movements; Experimental tools to measure movements; Robots for human movement interaction Joint mechanics: Actuation in human motion; Single joint neuromechanics; Voluntary movement; Multi-joint kinematics; Robotic analogy Impedance in movements: Muscle stiffness and viscosity; Joint impedance; Impedance modeling in limbs; Learnings from robotic analogy Redundancy in movements: Actuation redundancy; Kinematic redundancy; Redundancy resolution; Redundancy optimization in robots Movement dynamics: Forces at limbs and joints; Limb stability and motion; Dynamic redundancy; Learnings from robotic analogy Movement adaptation: Sensory feedback; Movement planning and neural control; Motor learning and memory; Learning models; Movement compensation Physical human-robot interaction (pHRI): Interaction perception; Interaction measurement and modeling; Interaction control - motion and impedance Human-robot collaboration: Movement rehabilitation - assistance and restoration; Performance enhancement

name: Biomedical Ultrasound
description: The proposed course will provide the conceptual foundations and practical insights for the use of ultrasound in imaging and therapy, encompassing physics, instrumentation, signal generation/processing, and clinical applications. Acoustic propagation and scattering: Wave equation and solution, plane wave reflection, refraction and transmission, scattering from objects and inhomogeneous tissue Ultrasound tissue interaction: Acoustic attenuation, absorption, cavitation Ultrasound sources: Piezoelectric effect, radiation from ultrasound sources, imaging and therapy transducers Ultrasound imaging systems: Hardware and Architecture, Software and signal processing incliuding beamforming, steering, image formation, speckle, artifacts Flow imaging and contrast agents: Color Doppler, Power Doppler, Microbubble Contrast Agents Therapeutic applications of ultrasound: Bioheat transfer, Hyperthermia, ablation, lithotripsy, histotripsy, ultrasound-enhanced drug delivery Safety, regulatory and ethical issues: Thermal Index, Mechanical Index, Image Guidance and Therapeutic Monitoring, As Low as Reasonably Achievable (ALARA) principle Recent advances in biomedical ultrasound: Discussion of Elasticity Imaging, Quantitative Ultrasound, Neuromodulation, and Ultrafast Imaging

name: Molecular Simulations – Theory and Applications
description: Introduction to computer simulation-Model System Interaction Potentials-Construction of intermolecular potentials- Studying small systems- Periodic Boundary Conditions-Potential truncation Classical Statistical Mechanics- Ergodicity- Common Statistical Ensembles Canonical Ensemble, Isobaric Isothermal Ensemble, Grand Canonical Ensemble- Transforming Ensembles- Thermodynamic averages, Fluctuations, Structural Quantities, Pair correlation functions, Transport coefficients, Long range corrections Monte Carlo Simulations - Importance Sampling, Metropolis Method, Monte Carlo Algorithm, Detailed Balance, Trail Moves, Isobaric-Isothermal Monte Carlo, Grand Canonical Monte Carlo Molecular Dynamics Simulations- The Idea, Initialization, Equations of Motion for atomic systems, Different Integration Algorithms and their optimal applications ( Verlet, Velocity Verlet, Predictor- Corrector and Gear Predictor Corrector), Common Thermostats ( Anderson, Nose- Hoover), Common Barostats ( Berenden, Parinello , Liouville Formulation, Lyapunov Instability, Computer experiments Analysis of Results – Liquid Structure, Time Correlation Functions, estimating errors in averages, fluctuations, structural quantities and time correlations- Correcting Thermodynamic averages Introduction to Advanced Simulations – Free energy calculation-Chemical Potential, Widom Insertion, Multiple Histogram, Non-Boltzmann sampling, Gibbs Ensemble, Configurational Bias Monte Carlo, Parallel Tempering, Replica Exchange simulations Introduction to Molecular Dynamics packages: Large-scale Atomic/Molecular Massively Parallel Simulator (LAMMPS) for Coarse Grained Polymer, Material Modeling and Solid State Atomic Simulations, Nanoscale Molecular Dynamics (NAMD), GROningen MAchine for Chemical Simulations (GROMACS)- Simulations of Proteins, Lipids and Nucleic Acids

name: Computer Graphics
description: Fundamentals: Review of Linear Algebra and Calculus, Projective Geometry - Coordinates and Transformations.

name: Variational Methods in Mechanics
description: Mathematical preliminaries: Basics of multivariable differential calculus, maxima and minima for a general function of n variables, constraints and Lagrange multipliers, examples; Mathematical preliminaries: Deﬁnition of a function, metrics and metric space, norm and vector spaces, function spaces relevant to engineering and applied sciences, definition of a ‘functional’ and its properties; Historical genesis of calculus of variations, classical problems beginning with the works of Fermat, Bernoulli, Euler, Lagrange, Jacobi, Weierstrass, Hamilton, Legendre and others, examples from the domain of applied mechanics, dynamics, thermal sciences, control theory, design optimization; Definition and physical significance of variational derivative, variation of a functional. Necessary and sufficiency condition for extremum or stationarity of a functional, the Euler’s equation, invariance of Euler’s equation.
