description: [1] Introduction: integration, processes, requirements, [2] Basics: overall project management, common technical processes, design, technical management, validation, verification, cost-effectiveness, [3] Project Life-cycle: software, research and technology, pre-phase concept studies, technology development, preliminary design, technology completion, final design, fabrication, assembly, integration, test, operations, Budget, [4] System Design Processes: stakeholder expectations, technical requirements, logical decomposition, design solution,[5] Product Realization: implementation, integration, verification, validation, transition, [6] Crosstalk: technical management, requirements management, interface, risk, configuration, assessment, key performance parameters, decision analysis.

name: Mechanical Engineering Laboratory I
description: Structured and Open-ended experiments in Solid Mechanics, Fluid Mechanics, Heat Transfer, Energy Systems, Dynamics and Vibrations, Selected Mechanisms, Control Systems, Automation, Manufacturing Processes and Metrology.

name: Mechanical Engineering Laboratory II
description: Structured and Open-ended experiments in Solid Mechanics, Fluid Mechanics, Heat Transfer, Energy Systems, Dynamics and Vibrations, Selected Mechanisms, Control Systems, Automation, Manufacturing Processes and Metrology.

name: Integrated Design and Manufacturing I
description: Introduction: design for strength and stiffness, economy, stresses, operation, maintenance, manufacture and assembly, transport, materials. Properties of materials, static, dynamic and impact strengths; factor of safety; permissible stresses;Material Selection: standards and selection. Joining techniques and properties; welding and bonding, screw and bolted connections. Introduction to tribology; Bearings and friction: Bush and rolling element bearings, Design selection; heat generation; properties of lubricants and selection; wear and associated failures. Shafts, axles and design of bearing mountings – stress concentration and thermal expansion. Design for fatigue. Introduction to manufacturing drawing including geometric and positional tolerance. Process planning. Rapid Prototyping. Design of mechanical components, sub-systems focusing on a project integrating design and manufacturing in a complete year-long Group Design Projects in Design-Test-Build mode.Intellectial Property Rights and Patenting.

name: Introduction to Manufacturing Systems and Metrology
description: Manufacturing Systems Fundamentals of Machine Tools: General-purpose, semi-automatic and Automatic machine tools; Introduction to Jigs and Fixture Design. Principles of location and clamping; Introduction to Computer aided design, Computer aided manufacturing, Basics of Computer Numerical Control and Computer Integrated Manufacturing; Modern trends in manufacturing automation – Industrial revolutions, role of sensing and robotics in manufacturing systems, IoT and introduction to predictive analytics. Metrology • Dimensional Metrology: Limits, Fits and dimensional tolerancing; Taylor’s principle, Gage tolerancing; Geometrical tolerancing of form, orientation, position, location, run out; Basic definitions and measurement principles, MMC/RFS conditions. • Comparators and Metrological Instruments: Principles of optical, pneumatic, electric/electronic instruments; Surface finish and its measurement, Coordinate Dimensional metrology. Non-contact vision-based inspection systems. • Introduction to assembly engineering.

name: Integrated Design and Manufacturing II
description: Introduction: design for strength and stiffness, economy, stresses, operation, maintenance, manufacture and assembly, transport, materials. Properties of materials, static, dynamic and impact strengths; factor of safety; permissible stresses;Material Selection: standards and selection. Joining techniques and properties; welding and bonding, screw and bolted connections. Introduction to tribology; Bearings and friction: Bush and rolling element bearings, Design selection; heat generation; properties of lubricants and selection; wear and associated failures. Shafts, axles and design of bearing mountings – stress concentration and thermal expansion. Design for fatigue. Introduction to manufacturing drawing including geometric and positional tolerance. Process planning. Rapid Prototyping. Design of mechanical components, sub-systems focusing on a project integrating design and manufacturing in a complete year-long Group Design Projects in Design-Test-Build mode.Intellectial Property Rights and Patenting.

name: Integrated Design and Manufacturing
description: Introduction: design for strength and stiffness, economy, stresses, operation, maintenance, manufacture and assembly, transport, materials. Properties of materials, static, dynamic and impact strengths; factor of safety; permissible stresses; Material Selection: standards and selection. Joining techniques and properties; welding and bonding, screw and bolted connections. Introduction to tribology; Bearings and friction: Bush and rolling element bearings, Design selection; heat generation; properties of lubricants and selection; wear and associated failures. Shafts, axles and design of bearing mountings – stress concentration and thermal expansion. Design for fatigue. Introduction to manufacturing drawing including geometric and positional tolerance. Process planning. Rapid Prototyping. Design of mechanical components, sub-systems focusing on a project integrating design and manufacturing in a complete year-long Group Design Projects in Design-Test-Build mode. Intellectual Property Rights and Patenting.

name: Principles of Dynamics
description: Fundamentals of analytical dynamics for particles, systems of particles, and rigid bodies, Lagrange's equations, Euler angles & Euler’s equations, Holonomic and non-holonomic constraints, Elements of linear systems theory, Single degree of freedom systems, Multi-degree of freedom systems, Distributed parameter systems (continuous systems).

name: Computational Fluid Dynamics
description: Introduction to Fluid Dynamics; Introduction to basic tensor analysis and the frame-invariant form of governing transport equations in fluid dynamics (Navier-Stokes equations) and heat transfer (steady and unsteady heat conduction equations); Classification of partial differential equations; The finite difference method, discretization schemes, truncation error, stability and convergence, and boundary conditions; Review of direct and iterative solution methods to a system of linear algebraic equations; Temporal discretization schemes – explicit and implicit schemes, treatment of convection and diffusion terms, one-dimensional and two-dimensional unsteady advection equation and advection-diffusion equation; Numerical solution of parabolic, elliptical, and hyperbolic governing equations; The finite volume method for one-dimensional and multi-dimensional domains, boundary conditions in the finite volume method, and the discretization of flux terms for non-orthogonal meshes; Numerical Solution of the Navier-Stokes equations (including the SIMPLE Method)

name: Combustion
description: Introduction to combustion, importance, applications, engineering issues; Laws of thermodynamics, chemical equilibrium, adiabatic flame temperature; Fundamentals of mass transfer, species conservation equation, Stefan problem, droplet vaporization; Gas kinetic theory, elementary and global reactions, reaction mechanisms, reaction rates, steady-state and partial equilibrium approximations; Hydrogen oxidation; Carbon monoxide oxidation; Hydrocarbon oxidation; Basic chemical reactors, constant pressure and constant volume reactors, well-stirred reactor, plug-flow reactor; Mass, momentum, and energy conservation equations; Laminar premixed flames, flame speed, flame thickness, flame speed measurement, ignition, quenching, flammability, flame stabilization; Laminar non-premixed flames, jet flames, counterflow diffusion flames; Droplet vaporization and combustion; Solid particle combustion

name: Industrial Hydraulics & Pneumatics
description: Introduction to Hydraulics &Pneumatics Comparison; Fields of Application; Review of basic fluid mechanics; Pneumatics:-Basic properties of compressed air; Generation &distribution of compressed air; Receiver. Conditioning of air- F.R.L unit; Actuators-Rotary & reciprocating; Types & classification; Rating; Selection based on Application; Valves- Pressure, Flow & Direction control valves Symbols Selection of valves; Piping; Synthesis of simple circuits; Illustrative examples of Application circuits; Design & Analysis; Truth table & Boolean Algebra; Hydraulics: - Pumps-types of positive-displacement pumps; Relief valve; Power-pack; Pressure, Flow & Direction control valves; Temp & pressure compensation; Actuators Accumulators & Intensifiers; Simple hydraulic circuits; Industrial circuits; Servo-hydraulics; Electro-servo hydraulics.

name: Advanced Fluid Mechanics
description: Governing equations of fluid mechanics; Equation of continuity, Momentum balance, stress and strain tensor, constitutive equations, Flow Equations in Moving Reference Frames. Lagrangian and Eulerian Formulations. steady and unsteady flows; vorticty vector and rotation tensor, vorticity equation, two-dimensional flows: stream function. creeping flow, Inviscid flow Introduction to stability, Kelvin-Helmholtz instability, Squire's theorem, Orr- Sommerfeld equation, stability characteristics of simple flows, transition, Turbulent Spots. Hairpin Vortices. Turbulent Flows. Classical experiments/observations, Kolgomorov’s scale. closure problem, correlations and spectra. RANS, Reynolds stress. Wall bounded and free shear flows. vorticity in turbulent flows, jet flows. kinetic energy budget, turbulent kinetic energy production and cascading, Eddy viscosity, mixing length, turbulent dispersion. Some insight into current activities for turbulence modeling.

name: Compressible Flow
description: Review of the governing equations of compressible flow and Thermodynamic concepts. Forms of energy equation for compressible flow. Wave propagation speed in ideal gases. Isentropic flow. Stagnation Flow Properties. Critical Flow Properties. Compressible frictionless flow in a shock tube and variable area ducts based on one-dimensional Euler Equations. Normal Shocks and Rankine-Hugoniot conditions. Oblique shock waves. Expansion Waves. Prandtl-Meyer Function. Shock-Expansion Theory for external compressible flow past bodies. Conical Flows. Internal compressible flow in constant area and variable area ducts with heat transfer and with friction leading to notions of Rayleigh and Fanno Flows. Combustion Waves. Condensation Shocks. Compressible Potential Flow Theory. Vorticity considerations. Notions of compressible velocity potential. Development of low-order compressible flow models for high speed flows based on linearized small-disturbance potential flow theory for subsonic, transonic and supersonic flows. Similarity Rules. Brief insight into hypersonic flow and rarified gas dynamics concepts. Disturbance behavior in unsteady compressible flow. Notions of Compressible Boundary Layers. Crocco’s Theorem. Shock wave boundary layer interaction. High temperature flows. Aerodynamic Heating.

name: Fuel Cell and Battery Systems
description: Introduction to fuel cells; fuel cell types, characterization, Fuel cell thermodynamics: thermodynamics review, Heat and Work potential of a fuel, Gibbs Free Energy, Predicting OCV, Nernst Equation, Fuel Cell reaction kinetics, Fuel cell charge transport, Fuel cell mass transport, Fuel cell modeling, Fuel cell characterization, Overview of fuel cell systems, Introduction to fuel processing, Thermal management sub-system design, Fuel cell system design. Role of batteries, classifications and types; Solid phase of porous electrodes, intercalate species transport; System response (time and frequency); Battery system models; State of charge estimation; Overview of battery management systems; Flow batteries: applications and challenges

name: Introduction to Robotics
description: Introduction to Robotics: Types, Terminology, Applications; Review of Coordinate Frames, Vectors, and Transformations in 3D; Rotation Representations: Matrices, Euler Angles, Axis-Angle, Quaternions; Rigid Body Transformations: Homogeneous Coordinates, SE(3), Composition; Denavit-Hartenberg (DH) Representation and Conventions; Forward and Inverse Kinematics for Serial Manipulators and Mobile Robots; Workspaces, Reachability Analysis; Velocity Kinematics; Manipulator Jacobians; Inverse Velocity Analysis; Singularities: Identification and Implications for Motion; Motion planning and trajectory generation using vision; Motion Control; Introduction to Robot Statics and Dynamics;

name: Fracture Mechanics
description: Review of theory of elasticity, indicial notations, balance equations, thermodynamics; Fundamentals of fracture mechanics and failure analysis, Griffith theory, solutions to linear elastic crack problems, stress fields at crack tip, energy release rates, compliance analysis and relations to stress-intensity factors, energy methods for estimating G and K; Small scale yielding, crack growth and stability; J-integral; Dugdale-Barenblatt model; Large scale yielding; Crack propagation; Micromechanics for failure analysis, Eshelby inclusion analysis, self- consistent methodology; Introduction to modeling mechanisms of ductile and brittle fracture; Case studies on computational modeling of fracture.

name: Energy Efficient Design of Separation Processes
Aircraft and Rocket Propulsion
description: Aircraft and rocket propulsion, history, background, and overview; Chemical thermodynamics, ideal gas mixtures, equilibrium calculations; Overview of gas dynamics, quasi one-dimensional flow through nozzles, shock waves, role of friction and heat transfer; Thermodynamic cycles; Air- breathing propulsion systems, overview, cycle analysis, and engine performance parameters for ramjets, turbojets, turbofans, turboprop and turboshaft engines; Rocket propulsion systems, thrust and impulse, vehicle acceleration, staging, space missions, cycle analysis, performance parameters, heat transfer issues, propellants, liquid rocket engines, solid rocket motors, hybrid rockets; Overview of engine components, inlets, nozzles, combustors, compressors, turbines.

name: Laser Processing of Materials
description: • History of lasers in manufacturing • Fundamentals of Lasers: Basic principles of Lasers, Types and classifications of lasers, Laser safety, Properties of Lasers, Applications of lasers, Basics of laser optics and optical components, Beam characteristics, Beam modification • Theory of Laser Processing of Materials: Mathematical modelling of laser interaction with materials, Energy balance during processing, Heat and fluid flows, Surface tension effects • Theory of Solidification: Solidification mechanisms, Microstructure changes, Residual stresses, Measurement of residual stresses • Laser-based Manufacturing Processes: o Laser-based additive manufacturing: Process considerations, Processes – Stereolithography, Selective laser sintering, Direct metal deposition (DMD), Laser direct casting. o Laser-based joining processes: Welding, process mechanisms, process characteristics, application to various materials o Laser-based material removal processes: Drilling, cutting and piercing o Laser surface treatment: Surface alloying, cladding, texturing; Chemical and physical vapor deposition o Laser-based bending or forming: Process mechanisms, o Other processes: Cleaning and marking processes, shock-hardening, biomedical laser processes • Sensing and automation in laser materials processing: Beam profilers, power measurement, optical sensors, in-process monitoring and control

name: Convective Heat Transfer
description: Conservation laws, scaling, dynamic similarity, laminar and turbulent convection, internal and external convection, natural convection and convection with change of phase. Problems and examples will include theory and applications drawn from nature and a spectrum of engineering applications; Introduction to convection, governing equations: mass, momentum and energy, scaling and similarity solutions, laminar external boundary layers, Falkner-Skan solutions, integral methods solutions, laminar internal flow, external natural convection, internal natural convection, convection with change of phase.

name: Introduction to Turbulence
description: Basic concepts: Definitions, Discussion about its relevance, Vorticity dynamics, Different scales in a turbulent flow: energy cascade of Kolmogorov and Richardson: Statistical analysis, part I: Ensemble averaging. High-order moments. Probability concepts. Random variables and estimators: Description of averaged equations: Reynolds equations, Closure problem, Eddy viscosity, Reynolds stress equations: Turbulence kinetic energy: Governing equations, Dissipation rate, Production, Transport: Homogeneous turbulence: Relevance, Turbulence decay: Free shear flows: Averaged equations, Free shear layer equations, analysis of ets, wakes: Wall-bounded turbulence: Boundary layers, Sublayers, Pressure gradient effects: Statistical concepts, part II: Autocorrelation, Integral length scale, Taylor microscale: Random processes and homogeneity: Multipoint correlations, Symmetries, Isotropy: Decomposition: Scales of turbulence, Homogeneous and inhomogeneous fields: Equilibrium: Signal processing an Spectral analysis: Analog to digital conversion. Modal decomposition, Window and filters.

name: Vapor Liquid Phase Change Phenomena
description: Introduction to Two-Phase Flows, Thermodynamics of Phase Change, Homogeneous and Heterogeneous nucleation, Surface Wettability, Boiling, Pool Boiling Curve, Heat Transfer Correlations in Nucleate Pool Boiling, Pool Boiling Crisis, Critical Heat Flux and Film Boiling, Flow Boiling Heat Transfer, Condensation, Nusselt theory of Filmwise Condensation, Dropwise Condensation, External and Internal Condensation, Numerical Modelling of Phase Change Heat Transfer, Equipment for Boiling, Evaporation, and Condensation.

name: Mathematical Tools for Mechanical Engineers
description: Introduction: Introduction to Systems and Processes in Mechanical Engineering; Overview of Mathematical Tools for Design, and Analysis; Linear Algebra: Applications: Examples from Vibrations, Control Systems, etc.; Scalars, vectors, and matrices; Special matrices; Matrix operations; Rank and determinant, Representation of linear systems of equations; Gaussian elimination; Matrix inversion; Vector spaces, inner product of vectors and Linear dependence, Singular Value Decomposition; Eigenvalues and Eigenvectors: Applications of eigenvalues and eigenvectors; Eigenvalue decomposition; Change of basis and similarity transformations; Diagonalization. Ordinary Differential Equations: Applications of ODEs in ME; First-order differential equations: Methods of solution: separable equations, linear equations, integrating factors, exact solutions; Initial Value Problems, Boundary Value Problems and Set of ODEs; Second and higher-order linear differential equations: Homogeneous and non-homogeneous equations, Complementary and Particular Solutions, Method of undetermined coefficients and variation of parameters; Systems of ODEs: Matrix methods for linear systems, Eigenvalue method for systems of differential equations; Stability and phase plane analysis; Solution by Laplace transforms Vector Calculus: Applications in ME; Vector and scalar fields: Vector-valued functions, Gradient, divergence, and curl, Physical interpretation; Parametric representation of curves, Line integrals of scalar and vector fields; Surface Integrals: Parametric representation of surfaces, Surface integrals of scalar and vector fields; Green's Theorem, Stokes' Theorem, Gauss' Divergence Theorem; their applications

name: Mathematical Tools for Mechanical Engineers-II
description: Partial Differential Equations (PDEs): Introduction to PDEs; Applications of PDEs in Engineering and Natural Sciences; First Order PDEs – Classification (linear, quasi-linear and non-linear); Solution to 1st order linear and quasi-linear PDEs using method of characteristics; Second order linear PDEs and their classification (Hyperbolic, Parabolic, Elliptic); Canonical Forms of Second order PDEs; Some useful PDEs in Mech. Engg. – Wave Equation, Heat Equation, Laplace and Helmholtz Equation; Introduction to Fourier Series, Eigenfunctions and their orthogonality; Solutions to Second order PDEs using Separation of variables and Eigenfunction Expansions; Initial and Boundary Value Problems in Second Order PDEs; Complex Variables: Complex Numbers, Argand Plane, Polar representation; Functions of complex variables; Limit and continuity of functions of complex variables; Cauchy-Riemann condition; Analytic Functions; Differentiation of functions of complex variables; Harmonic functions; Examples of some elementary analytic complex functions (Exponential, Hyperbolic, trigonometric, etc.); Taylor series representation of complex functions; Laurent Series representation; Convergence of series representation; Poles – definition and their orders; Introduction to complex integration – path integral, line integral and contour integrals; Cauchy Residue Theorem – Proof; Jordan’s Lemma; Cauchy Integral Formula with applications; Introduction to Branch points and Branch cuts; Introduction to Conformal Mapping; Integral Transforms: History and applications of various integral transforms; Review of Fourier Series (Part-I); Fourier Transform and Inverse Transform – definition; Some useful properties of Fourier Transforms (e.g., transforms of derivatives and integrals, translation, scaling, exponentiation, convolutions); Solutions to linear ODEs and PDEs using Fourier Transform – with application of Complex Contour Integrals; Engineering Applications of Fourier Transform (e.g., Data and Image Processing); Introduction to Laplace Transform – definition; Inverse Laplace Transform using Complex Contour Integrals; Useful properties of Laplace Transforms (transforms of derivatives and integrals, translation, scaling, exponentiation, convolutions, etc.); Solutions to linear ODEs and PDEs using Laplace Transform – with application of Complex Integration in selected cases; Brief overview of Hankel and Mellin Transform.

name: Accounting and Financial Management
description: Accounting as an information system for engineering decisions; Accounting Mechanics: Journals, Ledger, Trial Balance, Profit & Loss and Balance Sheet; Analysis of financial statements – Ratio Analysis; Introduction to costing – cost behavior - transfer pricing; Marginal costing including break even analysis, standard costing and variance analysis and budgetary control; Introduction to financial engineering; Sources of long- term funds- stocks and bonds and their valuation; Methods of project evaluation – payback period, accounting rate of return, net present value, benefit cost ratio, internal rate of return; Risk – Return Analysis : portfolio theory and capital assets pricing model; Introduction to interest rates, exchange rates and derivative instruments.

name: Organizational Behaviour & Human Resource Mgmt.
description: Behaviour in Organizations ; Personality & Its Measurement; Perception; Performance & Expectation; Attitude and Values; Motivation; Communication Process; Power and Influence; Ethics Leadership; Group Processes & Group Decision Making; Teams & Team Building; Conflict & Negotiation; Collaboration and Competition; Organizational Structure; Organizational Culture; Organizational Change Human Resource Management; Strategic Human Resource Management; Job Analysis and job Design; Human Resource Planning; Recruiting Human Resources; Selecting Human Resources; Employee Orientation and Placement; Career Planning and Guidance; Training Need Assessment; Employee Training and Management; Performance Appraisal: Concept, Methods and Film: Performance Appraisal; Performance Management; Compensation Administration; Incentives and Benefits; Employee Well- Being; Worker's Participation in Management; Managing Industrial Relations and Trade unions; Discipline and Disciplinary Action, Employee Grievances; International Human Resource Management Wrap up

name: Principles of Business Management
description: What exactly is “business management”? What does a manager do? Can management principles be applied to non-business situations? Is management an “art’ or a ‘science”? This introductory course aims to address some of these questions. It provides a broad overview of management and its functions. The topics covered are as below: Evolution of Business Management as a discipline; Introduction to basic concepts in Management; Primary functions of management: Planning; Organising/Staffing; Directing/leading; Controlling; Management in context: Small v/s large organizations, management in different cultural contexts; introduction to some “popular” management tools/concepts – strategy, core competence, re-engineering, centralization/decentralization.

name: Introduction to Marketing
description: An analytical approach to the study of marketing problems of business firms and other types of organizations. Business Marketing: process of understanding, creating and delivering value to targeted business markets and customers; Technology Marketing: conceptual frameworks and analytical tools for marketing decision making in high-growth and turbulent technology businesses; Marketing Strategy: integrative, dynamic view of competitive brand strategy.

name: Engineering Entrepreneurship
description: Introduction: A serial engineering entrepreneur’s point of view about starting a high technology software or hardware related company; Perspective of an entrepreneur: Why become an engineering entrepreneur; Cost of entrepreneurship: A realistic view of opportunity cost and loss of income; Preparation: What are the options for preparation; Starting a venture: A look at the steps involved and the choices to be made; inancing: Source of funds suitable for various type of start-ups; Managing and growing to company: Creating, capturing and protecting value; Build to last or that to exit: Is there a difference in strategies tailored according to exit plans?

name: Neuromarketing
description: Introduction to marketing and Brand management, concepts of neuromarketing, human brain and autonomic nervous system, attention and memory in brand communication, Experimental methods in neuromarketing, consumer decision making, the buying impulse, pricing, emotions, product life-cycle, case studies, hope and hype of neuromarketing.

name: Business Skills for Entrepreneurs
description: Field: This is an advanced entrepreneurship course for those who have already completed MS403. It is intended for students to develop an in-depth understanding of how to manage a start-up. It will be taught as a case study based instruction method. After students are exposed to the cases, the successes and failures will be analyzed in class in the context of the skills being discussed and students would be required to answer questions as class-work or homework. The cases may be presentations by entrepreneurs or written cases. In this first half of the semester, case studies will primarily focus on corporate culture, customer development, team building and negotiations, four essential elements of entrepreneurship. The second half of the semester will primary focus on idea generation, product management and financial planning & control. The course also allows students to start a new project or continue to develop their concept started in MS403. In addition to the instructors, visit by successful entrepreneurs, guest lectures and visits to start-ups will be organized to enhance learning.

name: Operations Management
description: Survey of analytical methods for planning and execution of operations. Topics include introduction to Linear Programming, operations strategy, capacity analysis, forecasting, queuing, inventory planning, supply and demand planning, total quality management, statistical quality control, and aggregate planning, scheduling and simulation techniques. The course focuses on meeting customer needs and requirements of quality, on-time delivery, cost, and service/flexibility.

name: Financial Considerations in Engineering Decisions
description: This course is designed to train students to develop an in-depth perception of major considerations in decision making involving large engineering projects. Students will be presented with tools of finance and decision making and interactively lead through practical applications. The course concludes with a case study involving a real world example. A primer will be provided on the technology relevant to the keystone case. Interpretation of Financial Statements: Concise review of corporate financial statements with practical applications from start-ups to mature corporations. Special emphasis on the interpretation of financial statements and the application of such interpretations to corporate decision making with emphasis on technology projects. Applied Decision Making: Introduction to decision making models with interactive exercises on canonical applications. Case study methodology: methodology review and in-class interactive case study by example. Review of Relevant Technology: The keystone case involves in its core, a technology milestone. A primer with examples and illustrations of that technology will be presented so that the students may perform an informed in-depth case analysis. The Case: Case study involving a major real life technology breakthrough and various decisions that lead to the accomplishment of the goal will be presented through a case summary. Students will be assigned different topics of analysis and would be guided through their independent analyses of the corporate decision making process taking into detailed consideration, the financial as well as technology aspects. As a bonus, the students will be given the opportunity to make predictions on alternate outcomes and their potential impact on the companies as well as society.

name: Managerial Economics: Concepts, Applications & Cases
description: Introduction: Theory of firms - Role of profit; Analytical underpinnings: Principles, tools, models, techniques, data structures; Decision environment: Internal and & external decision environments - essential elements of business and policy research; Demand analysis and forecasting: Theoretical underpinnings, demand functions and their estimations; Production and cost analysis: Theory of production, production functions, productivities, cost concepts and estimation, break-even-point analysis; Pricing policies: Pricing & market structure, pricing & objectives of firms, pricing & government policies; Money and finance: Characteristics of financial assets, sources & cost of capital, financial markets & development

name: Medical Products Industry:Quality and Regulations
description: Introduction: Theory of firms - Role of profit; Analytical underpinnings: Principles, tools, models, techniques, data structures; Decision environment: Internal and & external decision environments - essential elements of business and policy research; Demand analysis and forecasting: Theoretical underpinnings, demand functions and their estimations; Production and cost analysis: Theory of production, production functions, productivities, cost concepts and estimation, break-even-point analysis; Pricing policies: Pricing & market structure, pricing & objectives of firms, pricing & government policies; Money and finance: Characteristics of financial assets, sources & cost of capital, financial markets & development

name: New Technology Applications, Design and Business Models (NTADBM)
description: Link between designers, engineers and managers who understand the market needs; Strategic Design Thinking of such linkages and opportunities for creating entrepreneurial ventures; Development of new applications and business models around existing technologies for commercialization; A project based course with a focus on Customer-Design-Business Model Linkages and providing key insights on working in teams across diverse functions.

name: Foundations of Fintech
description: Evolution of the Financial Ecosystem: From Traditional Banking to FinTech, Overview of Financial Markets, Products, and Services, FinTech Value Chain and Stakeholders, Regulatory Landscape: RBI, SEBI, and Global Regulations, Digital Transformation and Innovation in Finance, Role of APIs and Open Banking, Payment Gateways, Wallets, and Digital Infrastructure, Blockchain and Distributed Ledger Technologies, Artificial Intelligence and Data Science in Financial Applications, Risk, Compliance, and Cybersecurity in FinTech, FinTech Business Models and Revenue Streams, FinTech Startups and the Venture Capital Ecosystem, Sustainability and ESG-driven FinTech Solutions, and Case Studies: UPI, Paytm, Revolut, Stripe.

name: Microstructural Engineering
description: Definition of a phase, thermodynamic criterion for phase stability, equilibrium between phases, Gibb’s phase rule, introduction to phase diagrams, potential phase diagram (e.g. temperature- pressure diagram of H 2 O), composition phase diagram and concepts of solidus, liquidus, solvus curves, tie line, lever rule, Introduction to various types of transformations such as eutectic, eutectoid etc.; Solidification: homogenous and heterogeneous nucleation, rate of nucleation, growth, isomorphous phase diagram (e.g. Cu-Ni) and solidification of alloys, Scheil equation, constitutional and thermal super-cooling, dendritic solidification principles, Eutectic phase diagrams (Al-Si, Ag-Cu, Fe-C) and eutectic solidification. Introduction to other phase transformations involving liquid phase such as peritectic and monotectic transformations; Solid- Solid Phase Transformations: Diffusional phase transformation; classical nucleation theory, growth, role of interfaces, spinodal decomposition. Partitionless phase transformations; massive and displacive/martensitic phase transformation; Thermo-mechanical treatments: Annealing, Normalizing, Quenching treatments, CCT/TTT diagrams, hardening and introduction to surface treatments, tempering, deformation induced phase transformation.

name: Materials Thermodynamics
description: Review of basic thermodynamics concepts; 1st, 2nd and 3rd laws of thermodynamics and their interpretations and applications; Statistical description of Entropy; Conditions and criterion for equilibrium; Thermodynamic relations and Maxwell’s relations; Behavior of gases; Single component systems; Clausius-Clapeyron equation; Solution thermodynamics (Raoult’s law and Henry’s law); ideal and non-ideal solutions; partial molar properties and chemical potential; Gibbs-Duhem equation; Gibbs free-energy vs composition diagrams; Composition-temperature diagrams; Construction of phase diagrams; Gibbs phase rule; Foundations of Computational Thermodynamics; CALPHAD method; Thermodynamics of defects.

name: Introduction to Computational Materials Engineering
description: Introduction: Role of computational techniques; Computational materials design and selection; materials databases Ab-initio methods and electronic structure calculations Molecular dynamics for modeling atomistic and molecular systems Monte Carlo methods Phase Field methods Finite Element Methods Multi-scale Materials Modeling Materials Informatics and data-driven approaches

name: Transport Phenomena in Materials Engineering
description: Introduction to transport phenomena Concepts of momentum transport: Newton’s law of viscosity and non-Newtonian fluids. Mathematical framework for Continuity equation, Navier-Stokes equations, Hagen-Poiseullie’s equation, Stokes law etc. Flows: laminar flow, turbulence, flow in porous beds. Applications of momentum transport in ceramic suspensions and polymer solutions, oil refineries, flow in porous membranes/electrodes. Concepts of heat transport: Fundamentals of conduction heat transfer, convection heat transfer, radiative heat transfer. Fourier’s law, steady and unsteady state conduction laws and governing equations. Heat transfer coefficient in convection. Radiation, Fick’s law of diffusion, mass transfer coefficient Applications of heat transport in materials processing namely heat treatment, solidification, casting, cooling of slabs, crystal growth, heat losses from the furnace, alloy homogenization, segregation, oxidation, reduction, microfluidics, cooling in electronic devices, flow and vacuum production. Concepts of mass transport: Fundamentals of diffusion; Fick’s first law and second law for steady and unsteady diffusion, uphill diffusion and Kirkendal's effect. Fundamentals of convective mass transfer; free and forced convective mass transfer, Convective mass transfer, rate laws and mass transfer coefficient. Application of mass transfer in case hardening of metals, doping of semi-conductors, homogenization, oxidation, absorption/desorption of gases in liquid metals.

name: Mechanical Behavior of Materials
description: Atomic-Pair potential and its relation to Elastic modulus, Hooke's law, Intro to Stress as tensor, Strain as a tensor, Tensor calculus and stress tensor in different coordinate system, Mohrs circle, Elastic Stress-strain relation for isotropic material, Elastic modulus as a tensor, Crystal anisotropy, Iso-stress, Iso-strain problems, Plane strain, Plane stress, Defects in Materials - Point defects, Dislocations - Structure, Burgers vector, Dislocations- Peierls stress, Stress field of Dislocation, Line energy, Frank's Partial, sessile dislocations, Lommer cottrell Locks, Interactions, locks, Pile-up, Strengthening mechanism - Grain Boundaries, twin boundaries, HP strengthening mechanisms, Solute interaction, Yield point drop, Luders band, Portevin-Le Chatelier effect, Dislocation/Taylor hardening, Precipitation hardening, Creep - Constant Load, Effect of Stress, temperature, Constitutive equation, Diffusion mechanisms, Dislocation mechanisms, Deformation mechanism Maps, Fracture - Stress concentration, Griffith's theory, K1 Fracture toughness, Fatigue - Low cycle, high cycle fatigue, Microstructure

name: Physics of Materials
description: Introduction, review of crystal lattices and bonding, Reciprocal lattice, Wigner Seitz cells, Brillouin zones. Metals: Electronic and Thermal Properties: The Drude (free electron) model, The relaxation-time approximation, Ohm’s law, Classical derivation of resistivity, Different methods of measuring resistivity, Classification of crystalline solids based on electrical measurement, Failure of Drude model, Electronic heat capacity, Thermal conductivity, and The Weidemann-Franz law, Hall effect; Sommerfeld model: The introduction of quantum mechanics, Fermi energy, The Fermi-Dirac function, Electronic density of states, The electronic heat capacity, Success and failure of the Sommerfeld model. Concept of phonons and specific heat, Einstein and Debye Models, Debye temperature, Applications. Schrödinger equation for a free electron (with no potential), Equation for bound electrons in a solid and its relevance to periodic potential, Nearly free electron model, Energy bands, Effective mass, Distinction between metals, semiconductors, and insulators, Applications. Optical properties, direct and indirect bandgap semiconductors, Applications. Superconductivity, Type I, and Type II, Meissner effect, Critical current and Critical magnetic field, Applications.

name: Structure of Materials
description: Crystal Structures – bonding in materials, crystal systems, crystallographic planes (directions and projections), Bravais Lattices; Crystal defects – point, line & planar defects, dislocation movement & slip systems, surface defects; Diffraction & Imaging – radiation-matter interactions, optical microscopy, x-ray diffraction, SEM, TEM, electron diffraction in TEM; Properties and applications – Mechanical, Thermal, Electrical, Magnetic, Electronic, Biological, Chemical

name: Material Thermodynamics and Kinetics
description: Review of basic concepts and combined first and second law of thermodynamics; condition and criterion for equilibrium; single component systems and Clausius-Clapeyron equation; solution thermodynamics, partial molal properties and chemical potential, Gibbs-Duhem equation, reference states, Gibbs free-energy vs composition diagrams, composition- temperature diagrams, tie line, lever rule, Gibbs phase rule; thermodynamics of defects; Concept of diffusion, self-diffusion, Fick’s first and second law, solutions to diffusion equation; atomic model for diffusion, Kirkendall effect, Darken’s analysis; concentration dependent diffusivity and diffusion in multiphase system, diffusion in crystalline solids, diffusion and crystal imperfections

name: Microstructural Engineering
description: Review of structures and defects; Concepts of grain recovery, recrystallization and grain growth. Differences between crystal, grains, sub-grains. Types of grain boundaries: low angle and high angle grain boundaries. Methods to measure grain sizes and lattice parameters; Definition of a phase, thermodynamic criterion for phase stability, equilibrium between phases, Gibb’s phase rule, introduction to phase diagrams, potential phase diagram (e.g., temperature- pressure diagram of H2O), composition phase diagrams (ex: iron-carbon) and concepts of solidus, liquidus, solvus curves, tie line, lever rule, introduction to various types of transformations such as eutectic, eutectoid, peritectic, peritectoid etc.; Solidification – homogenous and heterogeneous nucleation, rate of nucleation, growth, isomorphous phase diagram and solidification of alloys, Scheil equation, constitutional and thermal super-cooling, dendritic solidification principles, eutectic phase diagrams and eutectic solidification. Introduction to other phase transformations involving liquid phase such as peritectic and monotectic transformations, concept of undercooling; Solid-Solid phase transformations – diffusional phase transformation, classical nucleation theory, critical nucleus size, growth, role of interfaces, displacive/martensitic phase transformation; Thermo-mechanical treatments – annealing, normalizing, quenching. CCT/TTT diagrams, hardening and introduction to surface treatments, tempering, deformation induced phase transformation.

name: Material Characterization Techniques
description: Theory: Overview of the material characterization techniques and relevance; X-ray-based techniques (interaction of X-ray with matter, Electromagnetic radiation, X-ray production, Bragg’s law, order of diffraction, concept of absorption and scattering, elastic and inelastic scattering, form and structure factor, concept of allowed and forbidden reflections, multiplicity factor, Lorenz-polarization factor, temperature factor, fluorescence, and photoelectron generation, principle of operations and applications in XRD, XRF and XPS); Electron-based techniques (Interaction of electron with matter, secondary electron, backscattered electron, SEM, EDX, working principles using TEM, concept of diffraction, dark and bright field imaging, applications); Thermal analysis techniques (concepts of melting, crystallization, glass transition, oxidation, decomposition, effect of heating rate, sample size, environment on the DSC, TGA curves, applications); Surface analysis techniques (overview, surface morphology, roughness, wettability, surface energy, applications).

name: Corrosion and degradation of materials
description: Definition of corrosion – type of corrosion, economic impact of corrosion, corrosion science and corrosion engineering; Principles of degradation – environmental factors controlling corrosion, electrochemical reactions (half-cell, Anodic/Cathodic), atmospheric corrosion, Faraday’s law, Corrosion rates; Thermodynamic of corrosion – Chemical potential and standard states, Free energy and electrode potentials, Nernst Equation, Electrochemical cells, metallurgical characteristics important to corrosion, Galvanic corrosion, Crevice corrosion, Differential concentration cells, Pourbaix diagrams; Kinetics of Corrosion – Methods to determine corrosion rates, Electrochemical polarization, Electrode Kinetics for Activation Polarization, Mixed Potential theory; Mechanisms of Corrosion – Crevice and Pitting corrosion, Mechanically assisted corrosion, high temperature corrosion, Degradation of polymers and ceramics, Environmental factors affecting corrosion/degradation; Passivity – Electrochemical theory, properties and characterization of passive layer, passive layer formation for different surfaces (steel, iron, aluminum, alloys)

name: Mechanical Behaviour of Materials
description: Plastic deformation of single crystals – Crystal geometry/defect, deformation by slip, critical resolved shear stress for slip, deformation of single crystals; Dislocation theory – Burgers vector and dislocation loop, dislocation observation, Dislocations in FCC/HCP/BCC, stress fields and energy of dislocations, dislocation slide and climb, jogs, dislocation sources; Strengthening mechanisms – Grain boundary strengthening, yield point phenomena, strain aging, solid solution strengthening, strengthening from particle/fibers, strengthening due to point defects, martensite strengthening, strain hardening, cold- working, annealing, recovery/recrystallization; Fracture – Types of fracture, theoretical cohesive strength, Griffith theory of brittle fracture, fractography, dislocation theories of brittle fracture, ductile fracture, notch effect, fracture curve; Fracture mechanics – Strain energy release rate, stress intensity factor, fracture toughness, plane-strain toughness (K Ic ), plasticity corrections, crack opening displacement, J integral, R Curve; Brittle fracture and impact failure, notched-bar impact test, transition- temperature curve, fracture analysis, temper embrittlement, environment sensitive fracture; Fatigue of metals – Stress cycle, S-N curve, cyclic stress-strain curve, low cycle fatigue, strain-life equation, fatigue crack propagation, stress concentration, surface effects, cumulative fatigue damage and sequence effect, effect of metallurgical variables, machine design, corrosion fatigue; Creep and stress rupture – Time dependent mechanical behavior, creep curve, stress-rupture test, creep deformation mechanism, deformation mechanism maps, activation energy for steady-state creep, superplasticity, fracture at elevated temperature

name: Principles of Metal Extraction and Refining
description: Introduction, Hist ry and Importance of Metal Extraction; Mineral Dressing - Introduction to Mineral Dressing; Sizing; Comminution; Classification; Gravity Concentration (Heavy Media Separation, Jigging, Tabling); Froth Flotation, Magnetic Separation, Electrostatic Separation Metallurgical Fuels and the Energy scenario; Extractive Metallurgy - Pyrometallurgical Operations (Roasting, Agglomeration, Smelting, Converting, Refining & Secondary Refining); Principles of Hydrometallurgy; Principles of Electrometallurgy (Aqueous Solutions and Fused Salts);Flow sheet Design Based of Important Non Ferrous Metals Based on Materials and Heat Balance.

name: Advanced Materials
description: Polymers: types, structure, processing, thermos-mechanical properties, degradation, applications; Ceramic: structure, thermos mechanical properties of ceramics, processing of ceramics, degradation of ceramics; Composites: filler and matrix properties, manufacturing of composites, mehchanical properties of composites; Biomaterials: Introduction to biomaterials, types of implants (metallic, ceramic, polymeric), tissue response to materials, structure property relationship of biological materials. Requirements of biomaterials, degradation of biomaterials; Shape memory Alloys: martensitic transformation, applications

name: Design, selection and processing of engineering alloys
description: Introduction: Evolution of engineering materials, typical properties, microstructure control, structure- property correlation, life cycle analysis, recycling; Basic steps in alloy selection: Translation, screening, ranking and supporting information, material property charts (Ashby diagrams), including Young’s modulus- density, strength-density, modulus-strength, fracture toughness-modulus, fracture toughness – strength, strength-maximum service temperature and thermal conductivity-electrical resistivity relationship, relevance in design, material index; Ferrous alloys: Classification of carbon, low alloy, stainless and tool steels and cast irons, effect of trace (minor) elements, thermal processing - isothermal and continuous cooling diagrams and effect of annealing, normalizing, quenching, martempering, and austempering on microstructure and properties, surface hardening, HSLA, TRIP and TWIP steels; Nonferrous alloys: Classification of aluminum, magnesium titanium and copper alloys, effect of trace elements, thermal processing and resulting microstructure, step and interrupted ageing treatments, nickel and cobalt-based super alloys, recycling; Evolution of metallic glasses, composites and foams, high entropy alloys.

name: Materials Processing
description: Forging: classification, forging dies, metallurgical defects, forging equipment, powder forging and application; Rolling: fundamental principles, classification, cold rolling and hot rolling; problem and defect in rolled products, Extrusion: direct and indirect extrusion, hydrostatic extrusion, deformation, lubrication and defects in extruded products, Production of seamless pipe and tube, dynamic recovery and dynamic recrystallization, rod and wire drawing: defects in wire drawing, equ-channel angular processing related to superplasticity, sheet metal forming includes stretch forming and deep drawing processes; forming limit criteria and its application, spinning.

name: Physics of Materials
description: An overview of quantum mechanics, Electronic and crystal structure in crystalline materials, Reciprocal lattice, Crystal binding and elastic constants, Phonons and thermal properties of materials, Free electron Fermi gas, Energy bands, Metals, Semiconductors, Superconductors, Dielectrics, Ferroelectrics, Piezoelectrics, Magnetic and Optical materials, Non-crystalline solids: Thermal, Electrical and Magnetic properties, Applications.

name: Transport Phenomena in Materials Engineering
description: Introduction to transport phenomena, concepts of momentum, energy and mass transport, Newton’s law of viscosity, Non-Newtonian fluids, continuity equation, Navier-Stokes equations, laminar flow, Turbulence, concept of friction factor, fluidized bed, heat conduction, Fourier’s law, steady and unsteady state conduction, convection, heat transfer coefficient, radiation, Fick’s law of diffusion, mass transfer coefficient, Applications in macro and micro scale devices and materials processing; Solidification, casting, crystal growth, heat losses from the furnace, alloy homogenization, segregation, oxidation, reduction, microfluidics, cooling in electronic devices, flow and vacuum production.

name: Materials and Environment
description: Materials and Materials cycle, Biogeochemical cycles and its effect on environment, Introduction to sustainability, Mineral resources and their sustainable use, Principles of Metal extraction, Energy consumption in production and its sustainability indicators, Different types waste (solid, liquid, gaseous) and their management, Recovery and recycling of materials, Future demands of materials, Towards zero waste. Aspects of material pollution will also be covered (asbestos, microplastics, nanotoxicology).

name: Polymers, Ceramics and Composites
description: Polymers: types, structure, processing, thermo-mechanical properties, viscoelasticity models, applications; Ceramics: structure, thermos-mechanical properties of ceramics, processing of ceramics; Composites: filler and matrix properties, manufacturing of composites, mechanical properties of composites, Ply properties; Functional variants: Nanocomposites, ultrahigh molecular weight polymers, stimuli responsive polymers

name: Materials Selection and Design
description: Materials classification and materials hierarchy, materials tetrahedron, length-scale based materials design, structure property correlation; mechanical properties (fatigue, creep, strength/stiffness limited designs, fracture limited design) limited design considerations; degradation (durability, oxidation, corrosion) limited design; materials for electrical applications (defect engineering), materials for magnetic applications (structural based design considerations); design considerations for optical devices; design considerations for manufacturing/processing of materials (surface vs. bulk operations, microstructure evolution); end-use design consideration of materials (recyclying, remanufacturing, materials sustainability).

name: Introduction to Computational Materials Engineering
description: Introduction: Role of computational techniques; Materials design and selection and computational databases; a simplistic materials model: the Random-walk diffusion model; Ab-initio methods and electronic structure; Molecular dynamics simulations; Monte Carlo methods; Phase Field methods; Continuum Methods: Finite difference and Finite Element Methods; Materials Informatics and data-driven approaches

name: Corrosion and Degradation of Materials
description: Introduction Definition of corrosion; type of corrosion; economic impact of corrosion Thermodynamics of corrosion Electrochemical reactions (half-cell, Anodic/Cathodic); Free energy and electrode potentials, EMF Series; Nernst Equation; Galvanic Series Pourbaix diagrams; Electrochemical cells; Kinetics of Corrosion Electrochemical polarization; Electrode Kinetics for Activation Polarization; Mixed Potential theory; Examples Forms of Corrosion Uniform corrosion; Galvanic corrosion; Crevice corrosion; Pitting; Intergranular corrosion; Selective leaching Mechanically assisted corrosion – Erosion corrosion, Stress corrosion cracking, Corrosion fatigue Environmentally assisted corrosion- Hydrogen damage; Biologically influenced corrosion; Atmospheric corrosion; Soil corrosion Corrosion Environments and Corrosion Control Corrosion in acids Corrosion control – Design, Material selection, Coatings and inhibitors, Cathodic/Anodic protection Corrosion of Non-Metallic Materials Degradation of polymers and ceramics; High temperature corrosion

name: Materials Processing
description: Theory: Overview of various materials processing techniques; mechanical processing techniques (rolling, forging, extrusion, forming, joining); Powder processing techniques (powder mixing, compaction, and sintering); chemical processing techniques; electrochemical processing techniques, vacuum processing techniques.

name: Introduction to Quantum Materials and Devices
description: Overview and Relevance: Relevance of Quantum materials and devices, transition of Classical to Quantum realm, roadmap of IBM, Intel, Microsoft, Google, and various quantum industries to develop commercial quantum devices, NQM objectives, and government initiatives; Introduction to Quantum materials: Crystal structure and symmetry: periodic lattice and defects, Band theory of solids: review of basic concepts in quantum mechanics, Bloch wave, band structure, density of states, and quasiparticles, difference between quantum and classical materials. Introduction to qubits, superposition, and entanglement and various types of qubits: trapped ion, superconducting, photonic, electron spin, Quantum dots, Rydberg (neutral) atom, topological, and nuclear magnetic resonance. Quantum confinement, Van der Waals materials and their optoelectronic properties, Color centers in solids, Quantum dots and single-photon sources and detectors. Transition Metal Dichalcogenides, Topological quantum materials, Topology and Berry phase in solids, Topological insulators, Dirac and Weyl semimetals, on-demand topological band engineering, Correlated quantum materials arising from many-body interactions, Band theory limitation and electron correlation, Metal-insulator transitions, Mott insulator, Mottronics, Magnetism, Light-driven electronic phase transitions; Quantum Devices: Quantum optics, communication, and computation, Quantum effects visible at macroscale, Superconductivity, Josephson junction, superconducting hybrid systems, Topological quantum devices, single electron transistor, single photon emitter and detector photonic devices, Majorana fermion devices, quantum random number generator, Spintronics, photonic integrated circuits, atomic clocks, quantum well lasers, sensors, merger of classical and quantum devices, and brief introduction to the growth and characterization techniques of quantum materials and devices.

name: Material characterization techniques
description: Introduction to material characterization techniques; Structural Characterisation: X-Ray diffraction (principle, phase identification and quantification), Electron Microscopy (principle, morphology, crystallite size, elemental detection); Surface characterization: Atomic Force Microscopy (for determining topography) Contact angle (surface energy, hydrophilicity); Electrical characterization: Four probe measurement (for measuring sheet resistance), Hall measurement (for measuring sheet resistance, carrier concentration and mobility); Thermal characterization: Differential Scanning calorimetry , Differential Thermal Analyzer (to understand phase transition); Mechanical (Dynamic mechanical analyser for soft materials); Compositional characterization: (energy-dispersive spectroscopy, Inorganic content evaluation)

name: Surface Engineering
description: 1. Fundamentals on surface energy, surface tension, polar and apolar materials, viscosity, vapor pressure, enthalpy of vaporization 2. Surface properties: roughness, flatness, topography, chemical composition/surface chemistry, bond structure 3. Introduction to wetting, tribology, capillarity and biofouling 4. Types of fluids: pure liquids, emulsions, suspensions, Newtonian & non-Newtonian, biological and classification of real-life fluids such as blood, paints, ketchup, oil, yogurt etc. 5. Theories: Young’s equation, Wenzel theory, Cassie-Baxter theory, Zisman plot, van Oss-Chaudary- Good theory, Gibbs free energy minimization, van der Waals forces namely Debye, Keesom, London forces as well as hydrogen bonding 6. Surface modifications by chemical ways: dip coating, spraying, sputtering, silanization treatments, carburizing, nitriding, physical vaporization deposition, chemical vaporization method etc. 7. Engineering surface topography: micromachining, e- beam lithography, photolithography, laser ablation, chemical etching, curing, replication, hot embossing etc. 8. Surface characterization techniques: SEM, CA goniometer, AFM, optical profilometer, frictional measurements-oscillating tribometer, sphere on disc and pin on disc, Nanoindentation 9. Bioinspired strategies for tuning surface adhesion: Lotus leaf effect, butterfly surfaces, rose petals, Nepenthes Pitcher plant, gecko-lizard, underwater oleophobicity by fish scales 10. Tribology: Stribeck curve, lubrication, influence of viscosity, load, sliding speed on dry and lubricated friction and wear of materials. Archard’s wear principle and wear mechanisms 11. Applications and emerging challenges

name: Introduction to Polymer Physics and Processing
description: Polymer Physics: Conformation of polymer chains in solutions, melts, blends; thermodynamics of polymers solutions, blends; structure of glassy, crystalline & rubbery elastic states of polymers; entanglement effects, swelling & viscoelasticity. Polymer Processing: Manufacturing processes e.g. injection molding, extrusion etc.; mechanical & rheological properties related to particular processing techniques; flow models for processing techniques.

name: Computational Process Design
description: Computational thermodynamics; CALPHAD approach; numerical modeling of kinetic processes; Multicomponent phase equilibrium and phase diagram calculations; Simulation using Effective equilibrium zone modeling; Applications: metallurgical processes, alloy design, Recycling of materials and city waste, Extractive metallurgical process (Cu smelting, Ilmenite smelting, Al extraction and Coal combustion), Secondary steel making processes, Precipitation and mechanical properties prediction in alloys, Oxide calculations in different processes such as galvanizing and eco-AI/Mg process.

name: Science and Technology of Welding and Joining
description: Welding technology Fusion welding; solid state welding; brazing and soldering; mechanical joining and adhesive bonding; Physical phenomena during welding heat transfer and fluid flow in welding; chemical reactions in welding; residual stresses and distortion Weld metal solidification Grain structure and morphology; fusion zone; heat affected zone; phase transformation during welding Numerical simulation of welding Welding defects; post weld heat treatment; weldability testing; non-destructive testing of welds Joining of non-metallic materials.

name: Diffraction Methods for Structure and Stress Analysis
description: Structure determination: Symmetry of Crystals; Reciprocal lattice; Introduction to X-ray, Neutron and Electron diffraction; Structure factor determination for single and polycrystals with different symmetry (Cubic, Tetragonal and Hexagonal systems); Effect of crystal size and strain on diffraction; Electron diffraction and crystallography (Selected Area diffraction, convergent beam diffraction, Kikuchi lines); Diffraction for Crystallographic texture determination: Description of Orientation; Application of diffraction in Texture Analysis (measurement, evaluation and representation), Macro and Microtexture; Crystallographic analysis of Interfaces; Diffraction for Residual stress analysis: Fundamental concepts in stress analysis; Basics for determination of strain and stress fields by diffraction methods; Experimental methods for residual stress determination and associated Error analysis

name: Thin Film Processing and Characterization
description: Review and relevance of thin films, various thin film processing techniques, an introduction to vacuum science and Technology, Thin-film evaporation processes, Plasma and Ion Beam processing of Thin Films, Chemical Vapor Deposition, Non-vacuum thin film processing techniques, Substrate Surfaces and Thin-film Nucleation, Epitaxy, Microstructural characterization of thin films and surfaces, Interdiffusion, Reactions and phase transformations in thin films, Overview of various properties of thin films and their relation to their microstructures.

name: Deformation Behaviour of Materials
description: Solidification of metals, phase rule, equilibrium diagrams, Iron carbon diagram, phase transformation (austenitic to bainitic, pearlitic and martensitic transformation), heat treatment of steel such as normalizing, annealing and quenching (for hypo and hyper-eutectoid steels) and hardening of steel; Creep curve, effect of stress and temperature on creep behavior, stress- rupture test, deformation mechanism maps, high temperature alloys, fracture at elevated temperature, application of creep data for various materials, rules for the development of creep resistant alloys, difference in mechanisms for creep and superplastic deformation, factors responsible for high temperature design, creep- fatigue interaction; Fatigue failure, determination of S-N curves for both ferrous and non-ferrous alloys, effect of size, surface and metallurgical variables on fatigue, effect of non-metallic inclusion and mean stress on fatigue failure, low cycle fatigue, structural features of fatigue, effect of temperature on fatigue, fatigue crack growth, thermal fatigue and corrosion fatigue, certain practical aspect of fatigue failure,

name: Biomaterials for Tissue regeneration
description: Introduction to biomaterials, tissue engineering and tissue regeneration, principles of in vitro and in vivo studies, metallic, ceramic, polymeric and composite implant materials, synthesis and characterization of implants and implant materials, clinical use of biomaterials in cardiac, dental and orthopedic areas; tissue response to materials; structure property relationship of biological materials; requirements of biomaterials for use in cardiac applications, skin substrates, bone, ligaments and cartilage.

name: Process Plant Design – How to Set Up a Process Industry
description: Introduction to Process design, engineering design, process engineering & plant design; How to start process design; Process design development; Scope of project, process design & safety Materials and energy balances; equipment selection and specifications; Detailed plant flow- sheet;Siteselection;Economic evaluation of project; environment impact analysis;Planning tools – CPM &PERT;Case studies

name: Process Plant Design – How to Set Up a Process Industry
description: Recovery (static & dynamic ræovery), recrystallization (dynamic recrystallization) and grain gmwth; cold and hot working; fundamental concepts of metal forming, mechanics of metal forming, yield criteria; classification of forming processes; effect of temperature, speed of deformation and metallurgical structure on forming pmcesses; determination of strain hardening exponent (n), strain rate sensitivity (m) & plastic anisotropy (r) of materials and their combined effect on sheet metal forming with sp&-ia-l reference to formability; sheet metal forming processes (deep drawing, stretch forming etc); evaluation of forming limit diagram (FLD) of different materials; effects of grain size, temperature, cross head velocity and n, m & r on FLD; super-plastic fonning, super-plastic forming and diffusion bonding (SPDB), super- plastically formed components; bulk metal forming (forging, rolling extrusion & wire drawing); classification and advantages of different types of forging, forging dies, metallurgical defects of forging; fundamental principles of the metal rolling processes, classification of rolling processes, defects in rolled products, cold & hot rolling; extrusion, types of extrusion processes, variables of defomution pattern in extrusion, defects in extruded products, rod bar tube and wire drawing, defects in wire drawing.

name: Thermo-mechanical processing
description: Introduction to thermo-mechanical processing, strengthening mechanisms, heat treatment processes, fundamentals of mechanical working, different thermo-mechanical processes, residual stresses, defects, recent developments and new processes, case studies of alloy processing.

name: Thermodynamics of thin film growth
description: Introduction to bulk thermodynamics and its extension to reduced dimensions, Concepts of surfaces, interfaces and bulk, Methods for determining surface energy, interface energy and bulk Gibbs free energy, Concept of interfacial segregation, determining equilibrium segregation, Thermodynamics of phase formation in thin film under reactive atmosphere, Application of these concepts towards the growth of amorphous and crystalline thin films on metals and alloys.

name: Light Metal Alloys for Automotive Industry
description: Special light metal alloys for automotive applications; effect of alloying elements on alloys; controlled metal hygiene for load bearing components such as, chassis, wheel and inner body panels; cast and wrought alloys for engine and outer body panels; foil for radiator fins; heat treatment, mechanical properties (both static & dynamic) and microstructural aspects of alloys; surface topography and pretreatment; corrosion resistance property; flow curves; formability of alloys with special reference to forming limit diagram (FLD) at ambient and warm temperatures; thermal stability, safety and crashworthiness; life cycle data analysis; space frame technology; yield criterion; recycling of alloys based on chemical composition after end use; comparison between steel and light alloys with respect to the fuel consumption and environmental aspect; advance materials for automotive applications; in depth study on use of light metal alloys for automotive industries in India with a global perspective.

name: Interfaces in Materials
description: Solid-vapor interface: atomic structure of solid surfaces, surface energy, crystal growth from vapor and surface segregation; Solid-liquid interface: structure and energy of solid-phase interfaces, crystal growth from liquid, solute partitioning and morphological stability at a solid-liquid interface; Solid-solid interface: structure and energy of a homophase and heterophase interfaces. Growth of heterophase interfaces. Morphological stability and segregation at heterophase interfaces.

name: Advanced Engineering Materials
description: Introduction to traditional and advanced materials, their properties and applications, Origin of these properties, Electrical materials: metals, semiconductors, dielectrics, ferroelectrics, multiferroics, piezoelectrics, Optical materials: reflectors, absorbers, transparent materials, Optoelectronic materials, Magnetic materials: diamagnetic, paramagnetic, ferromagnetic, antiferromagnetic, ferrimagnetic, soft and hard magnetic materials, spintronics, superconductors, Biocompatible materials, Nanomaterials, Nuclear materials, Case studies: Advanced energy materials, healthcare materials, electronic materials, etc.

name: Structure and Defects of Materials
description: Crystallography & Crystal Structures – bonding in materials, periodic patterns, Lattices, Unit cells, Primitive & non-primitive unit cells, symmetry in 2 dimensions and 3 dimensions, point group notations, crystallographic planes (directions and projections), Crystal systems & Bravais Lattices, liquid crystals; Crystal defects & their significance – point defects and their role in materials processing, thermodynamics of point defects, Schottkey & Frenkel defects, dislocations, dislocation movement & slip systems, dislocation arrays and grain boundaries, dislocations in FCC and other structures, multiplication of dislocations, effect of dislocations on mechanical properties, planar defects, stacking faults, grain boundaries, twinning, surface defects with relevance to thin films; Diffraction – radiation- matter interactions, x-ray diffraction, structure factors, symmetry & reflection intensities, structure determination using xrd.

name: Kinetics of Materials
description: Thermodynamics vs kinetics; Homogeneous and heterogeneous reactions - chemical reaction control rate equation, reaction rate constant, reaction order, non-elementary reactions; Solid State Diffusion -Fick’s Law, mechanisms of diffusion, uphill diffusion, Kirkendall effect, steady and transient diffusion; External mass transfer -fluid flow and its relevance to mass transfer, general mass transport equation, concept of mass transfer coefficient, models of mass transfer -film theory and Higbie’s penetration theory; Internal mass transfer-ordinary and Knudsen diffusion, mass transfer with reaction; Adsorption –physical adsorption vs. chemisorption, adsorption isotherms - Langmuir, BET; Adsorption as the rate limiting step examples - gasification of C by CO2, dissolution of N2 in molten steel; Porous solids - specific surface area and pore size distribution; Reactor design -batch vs continuous reactors, ideal stirred tank and plug flow reactors; Mass balance in ideal reactors, residence time distribution; Models of industrial reactors; Electrochemical kinetics-concept of polarization, activation over potential, Butler-Volmer and Tafel’s equation, applications in electro- deposition and corrosion.

name: Thermodynamics of Materials
description: Open, closed, and isolated thermodynamic systems; state and process variables; extensive and intensive thermodynamic properties; first, second and third law of thermodynamics; condition and criterion for equilibrium; introduction to statistical thermodynamics; single component systems and introduction to potential phase diagram, Clausius-Clapeyron equation; multicomponent systems and solution thermodynamics, mixing process, ideal, regular and non- regular solution, behavior of dilute solutions, partial molal properties, chemical potential, Gibbs- Duhem equation; homogeneous and heterogeneous systems, Gibbs phase rule, composition- temperature phase diagrams, lever rule; thermodynamics of phase diagrams, reference states, free-energy composition curves, common tangent construction; thermodynamics of surfaces and interfaces, surface excess properties, capillarity effects on phase diagram, thermodynamics of point defects;

name: Characterization of Materials
description: Review and relevance of characterization of materials; Structural Characterisation: Optical microscopy (determination of microstructure and grain size ), Scanning Electron Microscopy (determining morphology, crystallite size, elemental detection, thickness), Transmission Electron Microscopy (microstructure, lattice parameter, substrate orientation relationship, thickness); Electron Probe Micro Analysis, Energy-dispersive spectroscopy and wavelength-dispersive spectroscopy (chemical analysis); Surface characterization: Atomic Force Microscopy (for determining topography), X-ray Photoelectron Spectroscopy (chemical characterization), Auger Electron Spectroscopy (chemical characterization, grain boundary segregation); Electrical characterization: Four probe measurement (for measuring sheet resistance), Hall measurement (for measuring sheet resistance, carrier concentration and mobility), Scanning Tunelling Microscopy (to understand local surface electronic structure); Magnetic properties; Thermal characterization: Differential Scanning calorimetry , Differential Thermal analyzer (to understand phase transition), Dilatometer (to measure thermal expansion coefficient); Mechanical and Thermomechanical characterization

name: Transmission Electron Microscopy
description: Introduction to TEM: Ray diagrams and review of the basic optics; Modes of operation of a TEM; Electron Guns; Thermionic source; vs Schottky vs cold-FEG; Gun Brightness and coherency of the source; Illumination lens system; convergent vs parallel beam illumination; apertures; Imaging lens system; Lens defects; spherical aberration; chromatic aberration; diffraction; astigmatism; Resolution of a microscope Scattering: Introduction to coherence vs incoherence; concepts of phase, energy, amplitude and cross-section; X-ray scattering; Electron scattering; elastic and inelastic scattering; kinematical and dynamical scattering; Born approximation for electrons; atomic form factor; comparison with nuclear scattering Inelastic Scattering: Introduction to electron energy loss spectroscopy; Instrumentation; Plasmon principles; General features of EELS spectra; Core Excitations; Energy filtered TEM imaging; energy dispersive X-ray spectrometry (EDS); electron-material interaction; instrumentation; artifacts; quantitative EDS and ZAF corrections; Thin film approximation; energy filters and chemical mapping; Chemical analysis with high spatial resolution Diffraction: Electron diffraction from a material; wave diffraction from a material; Reciprocal Lattice; Laue condition, Bragg’s law; structure factor and shape factor calculations; structure factor rules; Symmetry operations and forbidden diffractions; Superlattice diffractions; deviation parameter; Ewald sphere construction, Laue Zones; Tilting specimen vs tilting electron beam; Indexing of diffraction patterns; stereographic projections; Kikuchi lines and maps; double diffraction and forbidden diffractions; convergent beam electron diffraction (CBED) Imaging (diffraction contrast): Contrast in TEM; diffraction from crystal with defects; extinction distance; phase-amplitude diagram; Thickness fringes; bend contours; diffraction contrast from strain fields; Dislocation and Burgers vector determination; Weak- Beam Dark-Field (WBDF) imaging; Fringes at the interface; Moire fringes; Imaging of 1D, 2D and 3D defects; Introduction to Phase contrast (High resolution) imaging; Introduction to scanning TEM (STEM) mode operation; Various modes of STEM operation including bright filed (BF), dark filed (DF), annular bright filed (ABF), angular dark filed (ADF) and high-angle angular dark filed (HAADF); comparison of diffraction vs phase vs Z-contrast imaging in TEM

name: Semiconductor Materials and Fabrication Process
description: Introduction to Semiconductors and Fabrication: Basics of semiconductors and transistors, Moore’s law, an overview of the semiconductor fabrication process: from sand to device, future of chip technology with emphasis on the Angstrom era of semiconductors. Semiconductor fabrication: Front end of line process (FEOL): Oxidation, diffusion and ion implantation, lithography, etching, and thin film deposition. Semiconductor fabrication: Back end of line process (BEOL): Interconnects, chemical mechanical polishing, and packaging of semiconductor chips. Statistical and software tools used in semiconductor fabs: Statistical Process Control (SPC) charts, Process Control Statistical Analysis (PCSA), Klarity wafer map for defects and critical dimensions, Automated Process Control (APC), Structured Query Language (SQL), ScriptHost, LabVIEW, and JMP.

name: Advanced Alloy Design and Processing
description: Introduction to Alloy Design: Evolution of alloy design. Significance of alloy design in various sectors. Structure-property correlation, material property charts. Factor of safety and material index. Strengthening and Plasticity Mechanisms in Alloys: Mechanisms of strengthening, Strain transfer mechanisms. Steels: Fe-C phase diagram; Alloying elements in Fe-Alloys. Low-alloy steels, high-strength low-alloy steels, advanced high-strength steels, maraging steel, stainless steels, TRIP and TWIP steels. Alloys for High-Temperature Applications: Superalloys (Ni-, Co-, and Fe-based). Strengthening phases in superalloys. Eutectic Alloys and microstructure control in eutectic alloys. Cu alloys for high heat-flux applications. High Entropy Alloys (HEAs): Stability and properties, “Cocktail effect,” sluggish diffusion, and lattice distortion effects. Microstructural design and phase stability. Mechanical properties, corrosion resistance, and thermal stability. Lightweight Alloys: Al and Mg-based alloys-alloying strategies. Processing methods and their influence on properties. Ti-based alloys- Importance in aerospace and biomedical applications. Alloy Processing and Non-equilibrium Techniques: Advanced processing techniques- directional solidification, rapid solidification, powder metallurgy, additive manufacturing. Non-equilibrium processing and its effects on alloy stability and properties. Examples of advanced alloys developed through novel processing methods.

name: AI for Materials Engineering
